races.race_name: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Nursery Handicap

runners.name: Ponte Carlo
runners.spotlight: Upped in trip and switched to front-running tactics at Newcastle (7f, AW debut) last Thursday and he showed improved form in finishing third; he carried his head awkwardly last time and needs to transfer that promise back to turf, but the return to 6f and first-time cheekpieces may both help.
runners.comment: Good third from the front last Thursday (7f, AW); cheekpieces and return to 6f may help

runners.name: Koodini
runners.spotlight: Took a big step forward at Chester (6f, good to soft) on his third start and he showed it was no one-off when again finishing second last time, over this C&D (good to firm); cheekpieces go on; could give another good account.
runners.comment: Runner-up on last two starts and could give another good account in first-time cheekpieces

runners.name: Invincible Isaac
runners.spotlight: He's shown ability among his four starts, his best effort a keeping-on fourth of five here (5f, good) in April on his second outing; not ruled out in a first-time visor in his first nursery.
runners.comment: He's shown ability among his four starts, his best effort coming here; not ruled out

runners.name: Ziggy Starshine
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Bath (5f, good) in April and runner-up at Salisbury (5f, good to firm) and Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm) on next two starts; hinted last time that today's extra yardage would be a positive; chance.
runners.comment: Won on debut then runner-up on next two starts; the extra yardage could be a positive

runners.name: Yahaira
runners.spotlight: Left the form of her first three runs well behind to made all in first-time cheekpieces (retained) in a novice at Newbury (6f, good to firm) last Tuesday; could be well treated under a 6lb penalty on her nursery debut.
runners.comment: Made all in first-time cheekpieces in Newbury novice; could be well treated under penalty

runners.name: Havana Grey Star
runners.spotlight: Showed promise and inexperience when fourth on debut over C&D (good) in May; she hasn't kicked on since and was disappointing at Sandown most recently but it will be no surprise if she bounces back with a big run on her nursery debut; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Hasn't kicked on from promising debut but no surprise if she bounces back in first nursery

runners.name: Courseyoudo
runners.spotlight: He ran his best race yet when fifth of nine at Sandown (5f, good) three weeks ago on his third start, keeping on nicely; he could do with settling better now tackling 6f but otherwise the step up in trip may well prove a positive; firmly in calculations off the back of that latest effort.
runners.comment: Kept on nicely for 5th over 5f at Sandown and the step up to 6f may well prove a positive
runners.owner: Fairies At The Bottom Of The Garden Ltd

runners.name: Innichen
runners.spotlight: He's improved with each of his three runs (all 5f, good), finishing second at Newmarket in April on his second start then first at Pontefract later that month; makes nursery debut after a break and there's every chance that 6f will suit; could have more to offer.
runners.comment: Won at Pontefract in April on third start; returns from a break & could have more to offer

races.race_name: EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bearly Blue
runners.spotlight: Improved upon her debut run at Kempton (6f, soft) when runner-up at Goodwood (6f, soft) three weeks ago; she was no match for the winner last time but the third has since gone close; she's one to consider.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Goodwood on second start and the third has since gone close; considered
runners.owner: J Henwood, E Kelly And A Bright

runners.name: Fairy Well
runners.spotlight: 180,000gns yearling; second foal; Dark Angel sister to 5f/6f 2yo winner Rum Therapy (RPR 64); dam 5f-1m winner (including 2yo/US stakes), half-sister to Group-placed 6f 2yo winner Isle Of Fernandez; one of two pricey debutantes for connections; interesting to see how she goes in the betting.
runners.comment: 180,000gns yearling; one of two debutantes for connections; betting should be interesting

runners.name: Ash Tree
runners.spotlight: 240,000euros yearling; seventh foal; Blue Point sister to 7f AW 2yo winner Archer Royale (RPR 93), half-sister to five winners notably Takeko (Scandinavian 1m/8.6f including Listed) and Hot Team (7f-1m3f including 2yo Listed); one of two newcomers for connections and the betting could be informative.
runners.comment: 240,000euros yearling; one of two newcomers for connections; betting could be informative

runners.name: Grey Tamam
runners.spotlight: 140,000euros breeze-up 2yo; third foal; dam Listed-placed 5f-1m winner (including 2yo; RPR 99) from useful family; likely to improve for this debut run but she could still be a contender, especially if well backed.
runners.comment: 140,000euros breeze-up 2yo; could be a contender on debut, especially if well backed

runners.name: Nicely
runners.spotlight: 19,000euros foal, £95,000 breeze-up 2yo; third foal; Persian Force half-sister to French/Czech 7f-1m winner Josephino (including 2yo; RPR 102); dam 7f AW 2yo winner (90), half-sister to Group-placed 5f/6f winner Blockade; with a respected stable and this newcomer could give a good account.
runners.comment: £95,000 breeze-up 2yo; half-sister to talented 7f-1m winner; could give a good account

runners.name: Chiwara
runners.spotlight: Backed into 9-2 when runner-up on recent debut at Ripon (6f, good) behind the heavily odds-on favourite; open to improvement and she's a leading candidate this afternoon.
runners.comment: 9-2 runner-up behind odds-on favourite on debut at Ripon; leading candidate

races.race_name: Hon. Alderman Malcolm Wood Memorial Nursery Handicap

runners.name: Undercover Affair
runners.spotlight: He's run seven times and his sole win came in a seller at Goodwood (5f, soft) in June but that was a valuable race and he's continued to run well, beaten a neck in a novice at Redcar (5f, good) last time; tends to race prominently and he can make another bold bid in a first-time visor.
runners.comment: Won valuable seller at Goodwood in June and has continued to run well; can make bold bid

runners.name: Seed Ya Later
runners.spotlight: Went close on debut at Bath (5f, good) then won at Musselburgh (5f, good); not quite at that level when third in Bath novice (5f, firm) in May but she retains potential and could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Won at Musselburgh on second start; below that level at Bath since but retains potential

runners.name: Havana Gift
runners.spotlight: Improved upon her first two runs when keeping on for close second in a seller at Musselburgh (5f, good), having been outpaced; makes nursery debut off a realistic mark, assuming she can back up that latest effort.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Musselburgh seller on third start; realistic mark, assuming she backs that up

runners.name: Wait Geordie
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Bath (5f, good) in April then good third in warm race at Chester (5f, good) in May; well beaten at Beverley (5f, good to firm) last time but gelded since and could go well if returning to form.
runners.comment: Gelded since below-par run at Beverley and could go well if rediscovering previous form

runners.name: Past Passion
runners.spotlight: 4l runner-up at Ascot (5f, good) on second start and similar form when third of five at Ffos Las (5f, soft) last time; the return to better ground could be a plus and her pedigree provides hope that she can be better than her opening mark.
runners.comment: Has shown ability and pedigree provides hope that she can be better than her opening mark

runners.name: Holi Scarlett
runners.spotlight: Improved upon her first two runs when third of 11 at Bath (5.7f, good) four weeks ago, beaten just over 4l; she needs another step forward on this first nursery start, but that's possible.
runners.comment: Third at Bath last time; needs another step forward on nursery debut, but that's possible

runners.name: On The Queue Tee
runners.spotlight: Displayed ability at big prices in May on first two starts (5f, good/good to firm) then well beaten at Royal Ascot; today's nursery debut provides a more realistic opening but others arrive with more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Displayed ability on first two runs then well beaten at Royal Ascot; realistic assignment

races.race_name: Construction Day 14th October Handicap

runners.name: Al Maslool
runners.spotlight: Down the field on handicap debut at Sandown (1m2f, good) in June but bounced back six days later at Lingfield (11.6f, good), where he was runner-up in that novice contest in first-time cheekpieces (retained); he could still have more to offer for his top yard and he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Lingfield novice a fortnight ago; could still have more to offer for top yard

runners.name: Bergamo Gold
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row on AW in March and continued in good form on next two starts; well beaten on last two outings (turf debut last time) and, while she returns from a 45-day break and could get back on track at some point, others arrive with less to prove.
runners.comment: Two wins in a row on AW in March but heavy defeats on last two outings; 45-day break

runners.name: Emma's Letter
runners.spotlight: On a handy mark on last year's form up to 8.6f and it's possible the drop back in trip will help, but she's been beaten a long way on both starts this season (1m4f/11.4f) and has something to prove.
runners.comment: On a handy mark on her 2yo form but beaten a long way on both starts this season

runners.name: Ceinture d'Orion
runners.spotlight: She could do with settling better back up in trip on handicap debut but she's by Sea The Stars and the way she rallied for third at Ripon (1m, good), having been unbalanced on the undulating track, suggests today's distance could prove a good fit; cost 210,000euros in 2024; interesting.
runners.comment: The step back up in trip could be a good fit on handicap debut; interesting

runners.name: Red Rifle
runners.spotlight: He's raced four times this season and has suffered heavy defeats on three occasions, but two starts ago he easily made all at Catterick (1m4f, good to firm) and it might be dangerous to write him off.
runners.comment: Four starts this season; three heavy defeats but easily made all two runs ago

runners.name: Liveinthelight
runners.spotlight: Improved upon her first three runs for half-length win at 11-2 on recent handicap debut at Ffos Las (1m2f, soft), beating four rivals; cost 290,000gns in 2024 and every chance that she will continue to progress.
runners.comment: Won on handicap debut at Ffos Las and every chance that she will continue to progress
runners.owner: Lady Bamford , Mrs Collins & Bv Sangster

runners.name: Baron Wagstaff
runners.spotlight: Today's handicap debut provides a first realistic opportunity and he's been gelded since last time, with the step back up in trip another possible positive; however, improvement is necessary off his opening mark.
runners.comment: Today's handicap debut provides a first realistic opportunity but improvement is necessary

races.race_name: Watch Racing TV Now Handicap

runners.name: Mereside Princess
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row (1m, soft/good) prior to a clear, close second behind a well-treated rival at Musselburgh (1m, good) last Monday; she returns to action before a 5lb rise kicks in and she has leading claims.
runners.comment: Narrowly denied hat-trick at Musselburgh last week and runs before a 5lb rise kicks in

runners.name: Thornaby Annie
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice in a row (7f, good to firm) before winning at Wetherby (good to firm) recently on her first crack at 1m; she could have more to offer at about this trip and she's respected up 4lb.
runners.comment: Won at Wetherby recently on first crack at 1m; could have more to offer at about this trip

runners.name: Kameko Fever
runners.spotlight: Went close at Newcastle (1m, AW) in March and respectable fourth at Redcar (1m, good to firm) in April, but two lesser runs have followed and he needs to leave those performances well behind.
runners.comment: Went close at Newcastle in March but well beaten on turf on his last two starts
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Empirical
runners.spotlight: Reappeared over C&D (good) in May having been gelded and finished third in first-time cheekpieces (retained) on that handicap debut, despite being slowly away; he could be sharper for that outing and he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Third over C&D on handicap/seasonal debut in May and could be sharper for that outing

runners.name: Sahara Magic
runners.spotlight: She's dropping down the weights but has failed to finish better than sixth since winning on her debut at Southwell (6f, AW) last November and needs to raise her game over this new trip.
runners.comment: Hasn't kicked on since winning on her debut last November and has to raise her game

runners.name: Storm Esme
runners.spotlight: 2-3 in 2yo campaign (6f, AW/good) but a long way below that level on her four starts this year (5f-7f); the step up in trip needs to prompt a dramatic turnaround.
runners.comment: 2-3 in 2yo campaign but a long way below that level this year; steps up in trip

runners.name: Albertini Star
runners.spotlight: This is her first run since last November but her trainer had a productive June and she showed promise on the middle of her three 2yo starts, when a close sixth of nine at Leicester (1m, good to soft) in October; the step up in trip and first-time hood may be positives on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Handicap debut; lacks a recent run but her trainer had a productive June; goes up in trip

runners.name: Mayflower Billy
runners.spotlight: Second on handicap debut at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) in May and subsequent fifth at Newbury (1m, good to soft) came in a race that suited those ridden more patiently; has shown promise on good going; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Second on handicap debut at Doncaster then ran okay at Newbury; not ruled out

races.race_name: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Handicap

runners.name: Pay Attention
runners.spotlight: Slow starts can be an issue and he couldn't get into it when sixth at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) recently, but it wasn't a bad run and before that he was second at Newbury (1m, good); the step back up in trip may help and he might not be far away; the visor (worn on last two runs) is left off.
runners.comment: Slow starts can be an issue but he was runner-up two starts ago and might not be far away

runners.name: Beauty Generation
runners.spotlight: 0-12 on turf but ran well when fourth of 14 at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) in April and didn't enjoy a clear run when fifth of eight there (1m2f, good to soft) last time; has an each-way shout.
runners.comment: 0-12 on turf but ran well when fourth of 14 at Leicester two starts ago; each-way shout

runners.name: Wadacre Geisha
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has been runner-up in two of her three handicaps, most recently at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) last Tuesday when beaten a short head; runs off the same mark today and she's a leading contender.
runners.comment: 3yo who was short-headed off this mark at Beverley last week and is a leading contender

runners.name: Mohmentous
runners.spotlight: Won at Bath (1m, firm) in April on second handicap start and followed up at Redcar (1m, good) before 2l second of three back at Bath (1m, firm); there can be optimism that his latest defeat doesn't represent the limit of his ability and this step up in trip is worth exploring; key player.
runners.comment: Won two in a row then second of three at Bath; the step up in trip is worth exploring

runners.name: Phantom Shadow
runners.spotlight: 0-7 and his claims aren't compelling, but soft ground may not have suited this 3yo last time and there have been signs of promise previously; edging down the weights and things may click at some point.
runners.comment: 0-7 and his claims aren't compelling but things may click for this 3yo at some point

runners.name: Talking In Kode
runners.spotlight: Kept on for encouraging second at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) in May on her third start; down the field in both handicaps (1m, good) but it's still very early days and perhaps first-time cheekpieces will help.
runners.comment: Down the field in both handicaps but very early days and perhaps the cheekpieces will help

runners.name: Rugby Union
runners.spotlight: Showed little on first three starts but made handicap/stable debut at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm) recently having been gelded and showed promise in fifth; that was his first outing since January and he shaped as though he may improve for the run; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Promising fifth on recent handicap debut at Wetherby and could improve for the run

runners.name: Spec Of Light
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on stable debut at Southwell (1m3f, AW) in February but unable to get competitive since and needs something extra today.
runners.comment: Runner-up on stable debut at Southwell in February but unable to get competitive since

runners.name: Crimson Road
runners.spotlight: Hard to fancy on the evidence of the last 12 months but he was useful in his prime and he's one to watch in the betting off a reduced mark on his third start for this yard.
runners.comment: Hard to fancy on evidence of last 12 months but he's one to watch in the betting

races.race_name: Events At Nottingham Racecourse Training Series Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence Series)

runners.name: Miss Brazen
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good) in April on reappearance before close third at Beverley (5f, good to firm); unplaced on next two starts but she was slowly away when sixth at Musselburgh then drawn widest when fifth at Beverley (5f, good); she's one to consider.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in April and drawn widest when fifth at Beverley most recently; chance

runners.name: Beaumadier
runners.spotlight: Made all at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) in February and returned from a break with good third at Thirsk (5f, good) recently, when making the running under Hope Regan; could make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Made all in February (5f, AW) and returned from break with good third at Thirsk recently
runners.owner: R M Brilley, A E Townsend & Partner

runners.name: Winchurch
runners.spotlight: Two wins this season (6f/5f, good/good to soft), including here, and when fourth of five here (6f, good) recently he wasn't beaten far following an awkward start; major player if the stalls break isn't an issue this time.
runners.comment: Two wins this season & awkward start when beaten last time; major player if breaking okay
runners.owner: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd

runners.name: Spirit Of Applause
runners.spotlight: Ended his last campaign in good form and he reappeared with a respectable fourth at Beverley (5f, good) 16 days ago; he could show the benefit of that latest outing today and he's run some good races over this C&D.
runners.comment: Reappeared with respectable fourth at Beverley and could show the benefit of that outing

runners.name: Sam's Hope
runners.spotlight: Five AW wins since last September (6f/5f) and beaten only about 4l when ninth of 17 at Ascot (5f, good to firm) two months ago, when last seen; this is less competitive and she could be involved.
runners.comment: Ninth of 17 at Ascot two months ago; this is less competitive and she could be involved
runners.owner: D Morgan And K J Parris

runners.name: Hurt You Never
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who returned to form when third at Ripon (5f, good) in May and is 5lb lower today; however, this 7yo has struggled on both starts since and has something to prove.
runners.comment: Returned to form when third at Ripon in May but she's struggled on both starts since

runners.name: Muker
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner, the latest last July when 10lb higher; a market check is advised but this 8yo has been well beaten on his last three starts and needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: He's tumbled down the weights but this 8yo needs to turn things around

races.race_name: Horizon Group Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Scriabin
runners.spotlight: Exposed 14-race maiden over hurdles (placed on seven occasions) who has some fair form to his credit, including when second in a C&D handicap last month (good); likely to find a couple too good.
runners.comment: 0-14 over hurdles; placed seven times, including when 2nd in a C&D handicap latest

runners.name: Starshine Legend
runners.spotlight: Ex-James Ferguson; fair Flat performer (only win was in a 1m4f Catterick handicap on good to firm); quite a promising hurdles debut when third at Uttoxeter last month (2m, good; 10-1); improvement likely.
runners.comment: Fair Flat performer; quite a promising third at Uttoxeter on jumps debut; should improve

runners.name: Liberty Coach
runners.spotlight: Useful winner of four 1m2f handicaps on the Flat on soft/good to firm; not as good over hurdles so far, best run when third to two subsequent winners at Sedgefield last time in March (good); cheekpieces tried again (finished lame the previous occasion he wore them); player.
runners.comment: Useful four-time Flat winner (1m2f); not as good over jumps yet but may well do better

runners.name: Try Them
runners.spotlight: Bought for 7,000euros in May having shown fair form over hurdles for Emmet Mullins, best run when 7.5l fifth of 11 in a Limerick maiden hurdle in March (2m, soft); a possible on stable debut.
runners.comment: 7,000euros buy from Emmet Mullins in March having shown fair form over hurdles; possible

runners.name: Bogha Baiste
runners.spotlight: Has shown a similar level of form in three runs in novice hurdles this year, last time finishing fourth over 2m2f at Kelso (good); each-way chance.
runners.comment: Has shown similar level of form in three novice hurdles this year; has an each-way chance
runners.owner: A Lee, J Matterson, D Mullen & Partner

runners.name: Clanokre Lass
runners.spotlight: Fair form when sixth in a Cork bumper (2m, good) and fourth in a maiden hurdle at Clonmel (2m, good) this spring; improvement likely.
runners.comment: Fair form in a Cork bumper and a Clonmel maiden hurdle this spring; improvement likely

runners.name: Moon Reign
runners.spotlight: Showed fair form at up to 10.5f in three maiden events on the Flat for Joseph O'Brien; bought for 13,000gns in October; interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: 13,000gns buy after showing fair form on the Flat for Joseph O'Brien; interesting newcomer

runners.name: Quinnfinity
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in two mares' events over 2m4f this spring, the latest when tried in a tongue-tie; should improve on first run back after wind op.
runners.comment: Pulled up on her two starts to date but should improve on her first run after a wind op

runners.name: Dotties Promise
runners.spotlight: Kelso bumper winner last September (2m, good); beaten more than 50l in her three runs over hurdles; perhaps best watched for now.
runners.comment: Bumper winner in September; beaten more than 50l in her three runs over hurdles
runners.owner: Castle View Racing & W F Corbett

runners.name: Luckie Devil
runners.spotlight: Showed signs of ability in bumpers but hard to fancy having been pulled up on all three starts over hurdles.
runners.comment: Some promise in bumpers; pulled up all three starts over hurdles; hard to fancy

races.race_name: Kettle Produce Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Just Dottie
runners.spotlight: Four C&D wins, the most recent when justifying favouritism from 2lb higher in May 2025; Flat winner since (last September); reportedly never travelling when only fifth of 12 (Ravenscraig Castle third) here on latest start.
runners.comment: Four C&D wins; never travelling when only fifth of 12 (Ravenscraig Castle third) latest.

runners.name: Ravenscraig Castle
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (Class 5, good; same mark) last July; has mixed hurdling with Flat racing this year and wasn't beaten far when third over C&D (good; 7-1, Just Dottie fifth) 25 days ago; highly likely to give his running, even though he isn't a frequent winner.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when third to Scots Poet over C&D (good; 7-1) 25 days ago; one to consider.
runners.owner: A Sinclair, I Jardine & V Renwick

runners.name: Interchangeable
runners.spotlight: Naas winner (2m4f maiden hurdle) for Stuart Crawford in December 2024; 0-6 for this trainer, beaten 25l at Hexham (2m, good; 9-1) six weeks ago; mark continues to slide, now 11lb lower than three starts back; others are more obvious.
runners.comment: 0-6 for this trainer, beaten 25l (2m, good) six weeks ago; mark continues to slide.

runners.name: Tigress Lilly
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; no real improvement for wind surgery, beaten 13.5l over C&D latest; in good hands but still has plenty of work to do to reverse placings with Ravenscraig Castle (third) and Just Dottie (fifth); best to focus elsewhere.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden; no real improvement for wind surgery, beaten 13.5l over C&D latest.

runners.name: Pergamon
runners.spotlight: Solid hurdles strike-rate (4-7); seeks a hat-trick following wins at Huntingdon and Ludlow (both 2m, good; combined rise of 11lb); wouldn't want too much rain - good to soft would be fine - but otherwise holds a clear chance.
runners.comment: Seeks a hat-trick following wins at Huntingdon and Ludlow (2m); 4lb rise since latest win.

runners.name: Pure Gold
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who remains 4lb above her last winning mark (Hexham); low key in two C&D starts this year, beaten 19l and 17.5l respectively; this race is easier but she still needs to raise her game.
runners.comment: Low key in two C&D starts this year, beaten 19l and 17.5l; needs to raise her game.
runners.owner: Thp Racing & D A Harrison Racing

races.race_name: Secure Air Parks Edinburgh Airport Parking Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Garde Des Champs
runners.spotlight: Not a frequent winner but both his wins (from 37 jump starts) have been over 2m here; stays 2m4f and, after a fair third here last time (2m), is not without a chance
runners.comment: 2-37 over jumps; both wins at 2m but stays 2m4f; fair third here last time; a possible

runners.name: Gunnery Sergeant
runners.spotlight: Ex-pointer; lightly raced maiden under rules; fair run when eighth of 14 in a Kilbeggan beginners' chase in May (2m4f, good) and ran to a similar level of form in a maiden hurdle last time (2m3f, good to yielding); interesting on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Ex-pointer; maiden under rules; has shown promise and interesting on handicap debut

runners.name: Moodofthemoment
runners.spotlight: Has won four chases, all over this trip, the latest at Newcastle in October (good); fair third at Hexham last time (2m4f, good); a possible.
runners.comment: Four chase wins, all at 2m4f, latest in October; fair 3rd at Hexham last time; a possible

runners.name: Kinbara Firstdraft
runners.spotlight: Won two handicap hurdles for Stuart Crawford (2m/2m4f, good/good to firm) but yet to prove he is as effective over fences.
runners.comment: Won two h'cap hurdles for Stuart Crawford but yet to prove he is as effective over fences

runners.name: Wasdell Dundalk
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has won six chases, three over C&D on good/heavy, all in September; has shaped as though needing the race when below par in two runs this year (first was over hurdles); could go well.
runners.comment: Winner three times over C&D; not at his best this year but should come on for those runs
runners.owner: Abbadis Racing & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Button Rock
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form over hurdles for Gordon Elliott; bought for £1,800 in May and now goes chasing on his stable debut; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Ordinary form over hurdles for Gordon Elliott; sold for £1,800 in May; chase/stable debut

races.race_name: PWA Architecture Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Malangen
runners.spotlight: Tough veteran who has won 11 races, four of them here (2m/2m4f); has been running well recently, second over 2m at Hexham last time (good); player.
runners.comment: Tough veteran; has won 11 races, four of them here; comes here in good form; player
runners.owner: T Hunter & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Fairly Fulling
runners.spotlight: Won off a 7lb lower mark at Newcastle in March (2m4f, good to soft); fair run when fourth stepped up to 3m at Southwell last time; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Newcastle win off 7lb lower in March; fair run when 4th last time; each-way chance

runners.name: King Kodiak
runners.spotlight: Ran one of his best races since going handicapping when second to Betteryouthanme (who's now 4lb worse off for just over 1l) over 2m at Hexham last time (good; 33-1); has run creditably at this trip; a possible.
runners.comment: Ran one of his best races when 2nd in 2m Hexham h'cap last time (33-1); a possible

runners.name: Sonyourastar
runners.spotlight: 0-6 pointing in Ireland; has not shown a great deal over hurdles for Olly Murphy, although the form of his first race (only go at 2m4f) has worked out quite well, so worth considering on his handicap debut with tongue-tie now tried.
runners.comment: 0-6 in Irish points; ordinary hurdle form, best run over 2m4f; interesting on h'cap debut

runners.name: Top Flight Century
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off 6lb lower at this meeting last season when with Adam Nicol (good); has since won at Sedgefield (2m4f, good to soft) but has a bit to find with Betteryouthanme on recent Hexham form.
runners.comment: C&D winner here last July when with Adam Nicol; has won since, but below best last time

runners.name: Bayonetta
runners.spotlight: 1-15 pointing in Ireland but 0-16 under rules; placed nine times, including on latest start when 14l second in a Downpatrick handicap hurdle (2m6f, good to yielding); ran another creditable race when fourth over 3m2f here on previous start (good to soft).
runners.comment: Irish point winner; 0-16 under rules; fair runs last two, including 4th over 3m2f here

runners.name: Fourofakind
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner in 2024 (good); mainly disappointing since, including after wind surgery; however, stable is going better now and he's on a good mark; could go well.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner in 2024; mainly disappointing since; stable in decent form; could go well
runners.owner: Mrs G Jenkinson And Mrs B Butterworth

runners.name: Betteryouthanme
runners.spotlight: Comes here in good form having won over 2m here two runs back (good; beat King Kodiak by just over 1l); fair third off this 7lb higher mark at Hexham last time (2m, good); seems to stay 2m4f and should go well.
runners.comment: 2m winner here two runs back and fair third last time at Hexham; stays 2m4f; could go well

runners.name: Lenko
runners.spotlight: Has shown very little at long odds in his three starts, all at around 2m, but hugely unexposed and open to plenty of improvement now up in trip on handicap debut with cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: Has shown very little but interesting up in trip on handicap debut with headgear tried

runners.name: Breaking Ground
runners.spotlight: 0-7; well beaten on his last three starts, all here, the latest on his handicap debut when tried over 3m; plenty to prove.
runners.comment: 0-7; well beaten on his last three runs, all here, latest on h'cap debut; plenty to prove

runners.name: Dreamings Free
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over 2m7f at Hexham in May last year; well below form after a break on next two starts, all for Maurice Barnes; has had another break since and has come a long way to make his stable debut; hood now tried; a possible.
runners.comment: Fair form over 2m7f at Hexham for Maurice Barnes in May 2025; a possible on stable debut

races.race_name: Horizon Parking UK's Leading Parking Company Handicap Chase

runners.name: The Flying Poet
runners.spotlight: Profited from a final-fence mistake from Defying Gravity to make winning C&D chase debut (good to soft; 7-4 favourite, from 6lb lower) in May; didn't jump with fluency when runner-up at Worcester (beaten over 8l) next time; back from wind surgery, with headgear also tweaked.
runners.comment: Beat Defying Gravity over C&D (chase debut) in May; wind surgery and headgear tweaked.

runners.name: Burgundy Man
runners.spotlight: Kept on to account for Thehairyfella over C&D (soft) last October; fortunes have dipped markedly since and he weakened quickly when beaten 45l over C&D (hurdle) latest; wind surgery and an added tongue-tie could prompt a revival from what is a workable mark; consult the market.
runners.comment: Beat Thehairyfella over C&D (soft) last October; fortunes have dipped since; wind surgery.

runners.name: Cosmic Blizzard
runners.spotlight: Maiden (0-19); runner-up at Kelso (3m, good) last October but out of form since; in rear throughout over 2m4f at Hexham (beaten 27l) latest; it isn't easy to build a compelling case for his chance.
runners.comment: Maiden (0-19); in rear throughout over 2m4f at Hexham latest; others look stronger.
runners.owner: County Set Four & L V Russell

runners.name: Get A Superstar
runners.spotlight: Continued his progress, turning over an odds-on stablemate of The Flying Poet at Market Rasen (2m3f, good; from 6lb lower; fences in the home straight were omitted) three weeks ago; his win in a small field there the time before was over nearly 2m7f; looks hard to beat in hat-trick mission, if proving equally effective over 3m.
runners.comment: Continued progress at Market Rasen three weeks ago; 6lb higher, over a longer trip.

runners.name: Twp Stori
runners.spotlight: Best hurdles form (two wins) on soft or heavy; placed in two of his three starts over fences, albeit weakening at Uttoxeter (3m; beaten over 13l) 19 days ago; reportedly bled from the nose at Fontwell on penultimate start; task isn't impossible.
runners.comment: Placed in two of three starts over fences; rain favourable, but the task isn't impossible.

runners.name: Thehairyfella
runners.spotlight: 1-25 strike-rate; runner-up to Burgundy Man over C&D (soft) last October; underwhelming in two starts this year, beaten 47l at Downpatrick (3m, good) 19 days ago; blinkers are now added.
runners.comment: Runner-up to Burgundy Man over C&D (soft) last October; poor this year, so blinkers added.

runners.name: Defying Gravity
runners.spotlight: 0-8; scuppered his chance by making a final-fence mistake over C&D (The Flying Poet took full advantage) in May; unseated rider early over fences at Kelso next time and far from fluent back over hurdles at Cartmel latest; risks attached.
runners.comment: 0-8; final-fence mistake was costly over C&D (The Flying Poet won) in May; risks attached.

races.race_name: AEJ Facilities Management Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Loro White
runners.spotlight: Defied the market drift to make a winning handicap debut over 2m4f here (good to soft; in added cheekpieces) in May; despite being a beaten 11-10 favourite, she ran just as well when third over 3m (off this mark) at Uttoxeter latest; leading contender.
runners.comment: Course winner (2m4f; handicap debut) in May; perfectly respectable Uttoxeter third since.

runners.name: Creadan Grace
runners.spotlight: Narrow Down Royal winner (3m, good to yielding) last October, prior to career-best third at Ascot (3m;) the following month; less competitive in 2026, beaten 26l over C&D latest; cheekpieces fitted with renewed spark needed.
runners.comment: Beaten 26l over C&D (fifth) latest; cheekpieces are fitted with a renewed spark needed.

runners.name: Dalileo
runners.spotlight: Perth regular, with three course wins, including from 3lb higher in this last year; plugged on from an impossible position to finish a modest third over C&D (Creadan Grace fifth) 25 days ago; he's into the twilight of his career, so others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Last year's winner (from 3lb higher); could finish only a modest third over C&D latest.
runners.owner: Thp Racing & D A Harrison Racing

runners.name: Myfavouritesister
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden; beaten when nearly unshipping her rider two out over C&D on penultimate start; did too much in front, weakening from the last, at Hexham (3m, good; fifth of 11) subsequently; Brian Hughes is booked so, if ridden with more restraint, she isn't discounted.
runners.comment: 0-15; did too much in front, weakening from the last, at Hexham (3m; fifth of 11) latest.

runners.name: Les's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Continues to slide in the weights, now 10lb below his last winning mark (2m4f, Hexham); Kelso fourth (2m5f; in first-time blinkers) was okay but his latest Hexham effort (2m) was a poor one; plenty to prove, particularly over 3m.
runners.comment: Latest Hexham effort (2m) was a poor one; plenty to prove for now, particularly over 3m.

runners.name: The Best Way
runners.spotlight: Pounced late at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) in February 2025; steadily working back after a lengthy absence, with his Uttoxeter third (2m4f) last time a creditable effort; stamina reserves untested over 3m.
runners.comment: Uttoxeter third (2m4f) last time was a creditable effort; stamina untested at 3m.

runners.name: Maggies Boy
runners.spotlight: Won chase debut at Sedgefield (2m5f, good; from 2lb lower) in April but has posted form figures of PUP since; it's difficult to know what to expect, but he does revert to hurdles at an appropriate level and has run well here (second and third) in the past.
runners.comment: Lost his way over fences (1PUP); reverts to hurdles at  appropriate level; track is fine.

runners.name: Prince Nino
runners.spotlight: Had tumbled down the weights, with no win since September 2023, until springing a 66-1 surprise over 2m4f here (good to soft) in May; retains handicapping scope from this mark (5lb higher) but it remains to be seen if he's in the same fettle over 3m; chance.
runners.comment: 66-1 winner over 2m4f here (good to soft) in May; handicapping scope from 5lb higher.

runners.name: C'Mon So
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point; six-race rules maiden, soundly beaten in novice and maiden company (2m-2m4f) and faring no better - forward move petered out - on recent handicap debut at Hexham; tackles a new trip (rules) with Derek Fox back aboard.
runners.comment: Six-race rules maiden; forward move petered out on recent handicap debut at Hexham (2m4f).

runners.name: King Gold Boy
runners.spotlight: 0-7; pulled up in three of his last four, including at odds of 300-1 at Musselburgh (2m, soft) last December; hood added, but he surely isn't the solution off a 206-day break from 9lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: 0-7; pulled up in three of last four, 300-1 at Musselburgh (2m) last December.

races.race_name: Seadell Shops & Holiday Chalets "Hands And Heels" Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence Series)

runners.name: The Ubermensch
runners.spotlight: 0-9 but he's finished runner-up on three occasions, most recently when keeping on after being denied a clear run at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) six days ago; now steps up in grade but he's due to be 2lb higher and is a contender returned to this trip.
runners.comment: 0-9 but has been runner-up three times, including at Newcastle six days ago; contender

runners.name: Hibernate
runners.spotlight: Won here over 1m1f in April (good to firm) and followed up at Bath (11.5f, firm) next time off a 6lb lower mark than today's; disappointing at Beverley (1m2f) 36 days ago but he did sit close to a strong pace and may be able to bounce back.
runners.comment: Won here in April and at Bath next time; disappointed last time but may bounce back

runners.name: Currumbin
runners.spotlight: Both wins on the Flat have been over longer trips (1m6f/2m); most struggled since winning a maiden over hurdles last June and he was well beaten here over 1m2f in April; off since and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Both wins on the Flat have been over further (1m6f/2m); well beaten here over 1m2f latest

runners.name: Pleasant Man
runners.spotlight: Can lead; course winner who ended a lengthy losing run when making all at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) 12 days ago; a 3lb rise for that success still leaves him well treated on old form.
runners.comment: Ended a lengthy losing run when making all at Doncaster last time; up 3lb for that success

races.race_name: Weatherbys EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Nabati
runners.spotlight: 850,000gns yearling; colt by Night Of Thunder; second foal; half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Chicago Call (RPR 98); dam Group-placed 6f winner (96), half-sister to US winners Fashion Runaway (stakes-placed 6.5f dirt) and A Terrific Shot (6f/7f dirt), out of US 5.5f dirt 2yo winner; lots to like on paper and his powerful connections won this in 2024.
runners.comment: 850,000gns yearling; half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Chicago Call; lots to like on paper

runners.name: Shoof
runners.spotlight: Sent off 5-2 on his debut at Goodwood (5f, good) 13 days ago but faded as if the outing was needed, finishing last of five; steps up in trip with a hood applied and may take a step forward.
runners.comment: Sent off 5-2 on his debut but finished last of five; steps up to 6f with a hood applied

runners.name: Can't Stop
runners.spotlight: 75,000gns yearling; colt by Ardad; fourth foal; brother to 6f/7f winner Farough (including 2yo/KSA; RPR 83), half-brother to 6f AW 2yo winner Maui Breeze (76); dam runner-up 5f/6f (87), closely related to 1m2f/10.7f winner Fiji, out of dual 7f Group 3 winner; interesting newcomer and the market should be informative.
runners.comment: 75,000gns yearling by Ardad; brother to 6f/7f winner Farough; interesting newcomer

runners.name: Sovereign Dawn
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 5f 2yo winner End Of Story (RPR 90); showed little when last of ten on his debut at Windsor (5f, good to firm; 50-1) last month and a big step forward is needed over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Half-brother to a 5f 2yo winner but showed little on his debut at Windsor last month

races.race_name: Weatherbys Racing Bank Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Musical Times
runners.spotlight: Dam 7f-1m3f winner (including 2yo/Listed; RPR 111); strong in the betting and made a winning debut at Newmarket (7f, good; evens) 13 days ago, pulling well clear of the rest with the runner-up who had the benefit of experience; looks a nice prospect for her powerful connections and is the one to beat under a penalty.
runners.comment: Pulled clear with the more experienced runner-up when winning on debut; the one to beat

runners.name: Gracious Gift
runners.spotlight: Dubawi filly; sixth foal; closely related to French 6.7f-1m winner Fortuna Diamond (including Listed; RPR 110), half-sister to three winners including Arabian Light (1m-1m2f including AW; 114) and Sapphire Seas (9.4f-1m3f including AW/Listed; 108); dam 7f/1m winner (including AW 2yo/Listed; 103); newcomer with a smart pedigree and looks interesting even though William Buick rides Musical Times.
runners.comment: Dubawi filly; dam 7f/1m winner (including AW 2yo/Listed; 103); smart pedigree; interesting

runners.name: Aunty Patsy
runners.spotlight: 85,000gns yearling; filly by St Mark's Basilica; sixth foal; half-sister to winners Royal Scimitar (6f-1m including 2yo/Bahrain; RPR 102), Grigio (1m4f AW; 73) and Bushwacker (Swedish 6f dirt); dam unraced sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Alhebayeb, half-sister to 5f Listed winner Humidor; one to note in the market on her debut.
runners.comment: 85,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners including Royal Scimitar (RPR 102); one to note

runners.name: Skyglow
runners.spotlight: Filly by Space Blues; eighth foal; half-sister to winners Wetsand (7f/1m 2yo; RPR 86), Perisher (1m; 85) and Gnat Alley (6f including 2yo; 76); dam unplaced 1m 2yo (64), half-sister to 6f Group 1 winner Glen Shiel, out of 8.5f Group 3 winner; market may be the best guide on her debut.
runners.comment: Filly by Space Blues; half-sister to three winners; market may be the best guide on debut

runners.name: Attack Attack
runners.spotlight: Dam 7f 2yo winner (including AW/Group 3; RPR 104); played up before beating only one rival home her debut at Newbury (6.5f, good to firm) last month and finished last of ten after missing the break here over 6f two weeks ago; steps up in trip with much better needed in a first-time hood.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two starts, latest here over 6f two weeks ago; hood on; better needed

races.race_name: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap

runners.name: Man Of Desert
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden but his best two efforts have been over this C&D; probably didn't stay 1m2f here 35 days ago and has since been dropped a further 3lb; tongue-tie now goes on and he has possibilities back at 1m.
runners.comment: 0-11 but his best two efforts have been over this C&D; probably didn't stay 1m2f latest

runners.name: Great Mates
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in novice at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in November and she ran well on her handicap debut at Newcastle (AW) next time; strong in the market on her stable debut at Newmarket (7f, good; first-time hood) 13 days ago but proved disappointing; steps back up in trip and it will be interesting to see if her supporters return.
runners.comment: Sole win came on the AW for Michael Wigham; disappointing when well back on stable debut

runners.name: Tactical Plan
runners.spotlight: Both wins on soft/heavy ground over 7f in 2024; quite inconsistent since but did run respectably when fourth of 12 at Brighton (1m, good) 23 days ago; cheekpieces back on.
runners.comment: Quite inconsistent but he did run respectably when fourth at Brighton 23 days ago

runners.name: Big Alex Walmsley
runners.spotlight: Best effort when fourth in a maiden at Redcar (7f, good to firm) last August; well held in three handicaps since, latest on his reappearance at Lingfield (7f, good; first start since gelding operation) 31 days ago; hood added.
runners.comment: Fourth in a Redcar maiden last August but held in three handicaps since; hood added

runners.name: Sir Edward Lear
runners.spotlight: Both wins over 1m2f, the latest at Newmarket last August; now 2lb lower but he's been below his best in both starts since returning from a 194-day break and now attempts 1m for the first time.
runners.comment: Both wins over 1m2f; below his best in two starts this year; now tries 1m for first time

runners.name: Kalamunda
runners.spotlight: Five-time AW winner over 1m and he was runner-up on turf over this C&D two weeks ago on his first start for 270 days; the winner has won again since; has major claims in the returning cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Five-time AW winner but was runner-up over C&D last time; winner has won again since

races.race_name: Mark Sumner Handicap

runners.name: Luminous Warrior
runners.spotlight: Sole win when getting up late on over 1m at Kempton (AW) in December off 6lb higher than today; hasn't gone on from that and he's been comfortably held in his three starts on turf for this stable; others preferred.
runners.comment: Won at Kempton in December off 6lb higher but hasn't gone on from that

runners.name: Sporty Socks
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who was twice third on the AW last year but has been well held in five starts on turf, including here over 1m2f in April when last seen; drop back in trip needs to make a difference.
runners.comment: 0-8; finished third on the AW twice last year but well held in five starts on turf

runners.name: Midnight's Dream
runners.spotlight: Dual winner for Roger Varian (7f/1m; AW/good to firm) and he's now 13lb lower than for the second win; ran his best race since joining this stable when sixth of 18 at Doncaster (1m, good to soft; first-time visor) 18 days ago and has claims if building on that; headgear removed.
runners.comment: 13lb below his last winning mark and his latest effort was his best for this stable

runners.name: Shaws Phoenix
runners.spotlight: 17-race maiden who has beaten only one rival home in two starts on the AW since joining this stable from Alice Haynes; others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: 0-17 and well beaten in two starts on the AW since joining this stable; others preferred

runners.name: Campani
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Brighton (6f, good; first-time hood) last August and had the hood removed when coming home nicely to win at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in January; off for 122 days before finishing a creditable third over this C&D in May and has solid claims on his first start since.
runners.comment: Won over 7f on the AW in January; off since creditable third over this C&D in May; claims

runners.name: Give Me The Night
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; placed in a maiden at Nottingham (8.5f, good) last June but he's been well held in seven starts since; failed to beat a rival over 1m2f last time and blinkers are now tried.
runners.comment: 0-9 and failed to beat a rival at Nottingham last time; blinkers are now tried

runners.name: Mart
runners.spotlight: Seven wins (5f/6f), the most recent at Lingfield (6f, good) last May off a 13lb higher mark than today's; below his best 13 days ago and his stamina is a concern on his first attempt over 1m.
runners.comment: All seven wins over 5f/6f and his stamina is a concern on his first attempt over 1m

races.race_name: Branfords Handicap

runners.name: Giant
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been on the AW (7f/1m), the most recent at Chelmsford last August, but he returned to form at Windsor (6f, good to firm) ten days ago, finishing well to be second of 11; won't mind the step back up in trip and he's shortlisted.
runners.comment: All three wins on the AW but returned to form at Windsor last time; makes the shortlist

runners.name: Hakin Adraar
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; posted a solid effort when fourth of 13 on his stable debut at Kempton (1m, AW; first start since being gelded) in May but he was tailed off at Doncaster (7f, good) 18 days ago; bit to prove on grass.
runners.comment: 0-6; solid effort on the AW on his stable debut in May but tailed off on turf last time

runners.name: Mayflower Rock
runners.spotlight: Stepped forward from her debut when fourth of 12 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) in May but she didn't beat a rival at Wolverhampton (6f, AW; beaten 19l) one month ago; makes her handicap debut and may do better.
runners.comment: Fourth at Windsor in May but failed to beat a rival last time; makes handicap debut

runners.name: Adelaide Bay
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner on the AW in 2025 when previously trained by this yard and returned to some form on his first start since rejoining this stable at Southwell (7f, AW) in May, running on well for second; left the impression he's still in that form at Lingfield 28 days ago but turf is a slight question mark.
runners.comment: Shaped better in two starts since rejoining this stable (AW); turf is a slight question

runners.name: Jungle Knight
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the eighth attempt when winning on soft ground over this C&D three weeks ago (third start since being gelded); back up 3lb and likely has quicker ground to handle today.
runners.comment: Off the mark when winning on soft ground over C&D three weeks ago; back up 3lb

runners.name: Dion Baker
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who is thriving at present and won three of his last four starts, two of them over 7f at Brighton and the other at Bath over 5.5f; ran another solid race at the former track on Tuesday and can go well again.
runners.comment: Won three of his last four starts and ran well again at Brighton on Tuesday; can go well

runners.name: Wilde And Dandy
runners.spotlight: Both wins on turf have been at Doncaster (7f) on good to firm; close third of 11 at Newcastle (7f, AW) in December but ran below his best when last seen 147 days ago; break may have done him some good.
runners.comment: Third on the AW at Newcastle in December but below his best when last seen 147 days ago

runners.name: Dancing With Drums
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner over 6f, most recently at Kempton in May off a 1lb lower mark than today's; third on turf at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) after that success but he's been below that level over this trip in two starts since.
runners.comment: Three-time winner on the AW over 6f; below his best over this trip on his last two starts

runners.name: Federal Envoy
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been over 7f at Southwell (Karl Burke/Iain Jardine); has dropped a long way in the weights and took second on his stable debut at Ripon (6f, good) two weeks ago; steps back up in trip with solid claims.
runners.comment: A long way down the weights and took 2nd on his stable debut two weeks ago; solid claims

races.race_name: Cappawhite Civil Engineering 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Boston Benjamin
runners.spotlight: Narrowly won at Dundalk on Flat handicap debut in a first-time tongue tie (7f); below that level at the Curragh next time and gelded since; of obvious interest now hurdling given his Flat rating of 75 and connections; hood goes on.
runners.comment: Won AW h'cap bow; of obvious interest on hurdling bow with 75 Flat rating and connections

runners.name: Sofi's Gift
runners.spotlight: Holds a Flat rating of 48 after being well beaten in Flat maidens and on handicap debut; tries hurdling now but others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Rated 48 on Flat after moderate efforts in that sphere; others more appealing

runners.name: Words Unspoken
runners.spotlight: Australia gelding; purchased for 27,000euros as a foal and improved in value to 85,000euros as a yearling; dam French 7.5f AW winner, half-sister to Italian 1m2f Group 3 winner American Bridge, out of French 7f 2yo Listed winner; interesting profile; check market.
runners.comment: 85,000euros yearling; dam French 7.5f AW winner; check market

runners.name: Balance Of Trade
runners.spotlight: No wins from 12 starts on the Flat, leaving him with a rating of 48; well beaten in a Ballinrobe claimer last month; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 0-12 on Flat and rated 48; others make more appeal.

runners.name: Connor Of Mali
runners.spotlight: Big price at Thurles in a 1m maiden and ran accordingly; gave trouble at the start and withdrawn in two intended starts since then; goes hurdling now but others preferred.
runners.comment: Big price at Thurles on Flat debut and ran accordingly; others preferred

runners.name: Minella Rockett
runners.spotlight: Beaten a length and then a head in a pair of 1m maidens at Dundalk in November; too keen in early stages on handicap debut at Navan over 1m2f in May; well beaten in a maiden back at Dundalk last time; holds the top Flat rating (80) in this field and is courtesy of those efforts; big player.
runners.comment: Placed in AW m'dens and top rated on Flat (80) in this field; big player.

runners.name: Nicky Larson
runners.spotlight: Good start to handicapping on the Flat when runner-up over 1m at this course (yielding); okay fourth at Leopardstown next time (1m, soft to heavy); brings a Flat rating of 65 to this first attempt over hurdles and he could play a hand.
runners.comment: Good second on Flat (OR 65) h'cap bow here and okay fourth next time; could play a hand

runners.name: Swinging The World
runners.spotlight: Rated 44 on the Flat; modest form for Jack Davison and beaten 27l on stable debut at Gowran last time; unlikely.
runners.comment: Modest Flat form leaves him with rating of 44 on the level; unlikely

runners.name: Divelment
runners.spotlight: Placed twice in Roscommon maidens at Roscommon last year and again twice on AW earlier this year; claimed for 5,000euros after disappointing in Ballinrobe claimer in May; not too bad in a Down Royal maiden last time; rated 63 on the Flat and not discounted.
runners.comment: Placed several times in m'dens and holds a Flat rating of 63; not discounted

runners.name: Notahairoutofplace
runners.spotlight: Beaten 42l on debut in a Fairyhouse Flat maiden (1m4f, good); hard to fancy after that.
runners.comment: Beaten 42l on debut in a Fairyhouse Flat maiden (1m4f, good); hard to fancy after that

races.race_name: Cappawhite Civil Engineering Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Dairy Force
runners.spotlight: Kilbeggan maiden hurdle winner for Liam O'Brien (good to yielding) in 2024; mixed signals from his form for Gordon Elliott; never in the hunt at Kilbeggan last time after an early mistake; better judged on previous Bellewstown second.
runners.comment: Never in the hunt at Kilbeggan after an early mistake, better judged on Bellewstown second

runners.name: Eastmore
runners.spotlight: Held his form well after winning a Down Royal handicap last August (2m1f, good to firm); back from a break, he appeared to get outstayed by the front two over 2m3f at Cork; no match for an easy winner at Tramore; one of the main contenders here.
runners.comment: Has developed into a consistent sort but hard to win with, unlikely to be far away

runners.name: Inchidaly Copper
runners.spotlight: In good form before a winter break; useful sort, but has a definite preference for longer trips; four-time winner in points, most recent of five tracks wins was gained over 2m7f.
runners.comment: In good form before a winter break, useful but has a definite preference for longer trips

runners.name: Mo Ghille Mar
runners.spotlight: Last of three Flat wins for Jessica Harrington was off 84 last summer; was having her eighth hurdles start when making the breakthrough in a Wexford handicap in May; failed to back that up at Listowel.
runners.comment: Three Flat wins for Jessica Harrington, failed to back up Wexford hurdles win at Listowel

runners.name: Harpy Eagle
runners.spotlight: Some good placed efforts in maidens on good ground; placed three times between August and October last year; modest winter form; better ground should suit and the addition of cheekpieces may help.
runners.comment: Maiden was placed three times between August and October last year, modest winter form

runners.name: Regards To Rose
runners.spotlight: Fairly consistent before winning a 2m handicap hurdle at the Listowel festival, an event well targeted by her trainer; probably needed Ballinrobe run in May.
runners.comment: Fairly consistent before winning at Listowel last autumn, probably needed Ballinrobe run
runners.owner: Exors of the Late M Mortell

runners.name: Youlita
runners.spotlight: Downpatrick maiden hurdle winner in May 2025; fair form in novice hurdles since; travelled well before finding little when sixth on handicap debut at Down Royal; winning chase debut at Clonmel last time; merits respect, worth checking his market position relative to the stable's Eastmore.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner on good ground, winning chase debut last time, merits respect

runners.name: Bop Wont Stop
runners.spotlight: Not a bad effort at Cork in May after a long absence; confidence dented by Listowel run last month, looked a difficult ride, pulling hard at times and hanging.
runners.comment: Not a bad effort at Cork in May after a long absence, confidence dented by Listowel run

runners.name: Tuche De Houelle
runners.spotlight: French import; won a 2m2f hurdle race on good ground in May last year; an interesting recruit to a leading local yard; has a big burden, but Calum Hogan takes off a useful 5lb; worth a market check.
runners.comment: French import, an interesting recruit to a leading local yard, worth a market check

runners.name: Emesteraye
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in three maidens; solid effort on handicap/seasonal debut at Wexford, finished third despite showing a tendency to hang left; young mare, can improve.
runners.comment: Showed a tendency to hang left when a fair third at Wexford on handicap debut, can improve

runners.name: Silly Mongoose
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in bumpers; began over hurdles with a low-key Wexford run; much improved when fifth at Clonmel; won well at Kilbeggan despite racing keenly; opening mark gives him a fair chance.
runners.comment: Won a Kilbeggan maiden well despite racing keenly, opening mark gives him a fair chance

runners.name: Northern Ruler
runners.spotlight: AW winner for Karl Burke early last year; regressive in turf handicaps; hinted at handicap potential at Naas in March on his third maiden hurdle start, beaten 28l in sixth behind a Willie Mullins-trained odds-on chance in a 20-runner contest.
runners.comment: AW winner, hinted at handicap potential at Naas in March on his third maiden hurdle start

races.race_name: Kevin McManus Bookmaker Grimes Hurdle (Grade 3)

runners.name: Bowensonfire
runners.spotlight: Admirably consistent handicapper who brought his tally to five with two wins at Leopardstown last winter; ran in the County Hurdle before an ambitious Grade 2 attempt at Fairyhouse; has won on good ground, would probably prefer an ease.
runners.comment: Admirably consistent handicapper, has won on good ground, would probably prefer an ease

runners.name: Daddy Long Legs
runners.spotlight: Course Grade 3 winner in 2024, currently rated 150 after Ballinrobe win in May; previously may have needed the run when well beaten in a Southwell race won by Constitution Hill; beaten by the unconsidered Counting Coup in a Flat maiden at Down Royal 12 days ago; stylish Ballinrobe success in first-time cheekpieces makes him the one to beat at these weights.
runners.comment: Good Ballinrobe win in first-time cheekpieces makes him the one to beat at these weights

runners.name: King Of Kingsfield
runners.spotlight: Useful chasing campaign last season returned three wins from seven starts; not at his best in a Newbury Grade 2 last November or in Aintree Grade 1 in April; 140 hurdles rating puts him 8lb behind Bowensfire. might be more effective than his stablemate on this ground.
runners.comment: Useful chasing campaign last season, 140 hurdles rating puts him 8lb behind Bowensfire,

runners.name: Queenofthelodge
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicaps in the first half of last season; gained a third handicap success in 2m4f Clonmel mares' event in October; was found wanting in conditions races later; tough task in this company; cheekpieces on for the first time.
runners.comment: Progressive in handicaps in first half of last season, found wanting in conditions races

runners.name: Jesse Evans
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner; now 2-10 over fences having made all to score readily over 2m4f Listowel a month ago; won this race last year as a prelude to a fine effort in taking second place in the Galway Plate; faces a tough task this time, since he meets Daddy Long Legs on 12lb worse than handicap terms.
runners.comment: Won this race last year as prelude to Galway Plate second, not an easy task at the weights

runners.name: Barbizon
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat for John Murphy, 2-4 and rated 101; failed to prosper as a juvenile hurdler for this stable after a winning introduction at Navan last November; completely tailed off in the Fred Winter; best watched.
runners.comment: Useful on Flat for John Murphy, failed to prosper as a juvenile hurdler after a maiden win

runners.name: Run For Oscar
runners.spotlight: Smart dual-purpose performer, although he has been lightly raced since taking a 3m1f Kilbeggan conditions hurdle in August 2024; absent since last October; would receive 16lb from Daddy Long Legs in a handicap.
runners.comment: Smart dual-purpose performer, lacks a recent run, rated 16lb inferior to Daddy Long Legs

races.race_name: Eddie Noonan Groundworks Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Latopix
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m4f in France; modest form in two bumper starts in Britain; failed to build on promising hurdling debut third at Gowran (2m, heavy) when tailed off at Navan in December (2m, soft to heavy); leading chance now based on last month's claimer-ridden Punchestown second.
runners.comment: Winner over 1m4f in France, leading chance here based on last month's Punchestown second

runners.name: Jack Owen Jack
runners.spotlight: Solid efforts in bumpers; runner-up at Cork last October (2m3f, good) and when close third at Kilbeggan in May (good);  can feature if jumping better than at Listowel, when he was 4l third, around 2l behind the runner-up, a stablemate of Latopix.
runners.comment: Placed all three starts, twice in bumpers, can feature if jumping better than at Listowel

runners.name: Open Approach
runners.spotlight: Fair pedigree; only 5-1 for debut at Clonmel, carried out at the second but continued to finish in rear; looked like a staying type when a rallying fourth in a modest 2m6f maiden at Downpatrick
runners.comment: Looked like a staying type when a rallying fourth in a modest 2m6f maiden at Downpatrick

runners.name: Goodmancon
runners.spotlight: Similar profile to yard's Lord Lackendarra, well exposed in bumpers; fair hurdle run at Tramore in April; failed to build on that at Killarney the following month.
runners.comment: Similar profile to yard's Lord Lackendarra, well exposed in bumpers, one fair hurdle run

runners.name: Freddie's Back
runners.spotlight: Won his sole point-to-point in May 2024; shown ability in making the frame in two outings at this track last season; however, a reported history of veterinary issues is a concern.
runners.comment: Point winner, has shown ability at this track, history of veterinary issues is a concern

runners.name: Siege Of Carthage
runners.spotlight: Getaway 6yo whose dam was a 2m4f hurdle winner, closely related to high-class staying chaser Conflated; has shown some ability in bumpers; chased home an easy winner at Gowran in March; soundly beaten in fifth at Wexford on second attempt.
runners.comment: Smart pedigree, has shown ability in bumpers, may need to obtain hurdling experience

runners.name: Torque De Lune
runners.spotlight: Huge price when showing mild promise with a recent fourth placing at Downpatrick; plenty more improvement needed.
runners.comment: Huge price when showing mild promise with a recent fourth placing at Downpatrick

runners.name: Gc Star Speed
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; half-sister to a point/2m hurdle winner and a point winner; dam unraced sister to bumper/useful hurdler winner Grangeclare Lark, half-sister to smart hurdle/chase winner Scarthy Lad; set to make debut at the age of eight; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, dam comes from a good family, set to make debut at the age of eight

runners.name: Calzaghi
runners.spotlight: Won a Flat handicap at Navan in 2024; soundly beaten in two outings over hurdles; not a likely maiden winner at this stage.
runners.comment: Won a Flat handicap at Navan in 2024, soundly beaten in two outings over hurdles

runners.name: Lord Lackendarra
runners.spotlight: Placed in three of his 12 starts in bumpers, moderate third in a maiden hurdle at Tramore on his penultimate start.
runners.comment: Placed in three of his 12 starts in bumpers, moderate third in a maiden hurdle at Tramore

runners.name: Boomerang Breeze
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; half-brother to winners Queen Of Cool, Hintsnallegations, All That Remains and Tartan Cookie; dam 1m Flat/hurdle winner.
runners.comment: Tenth foal; half-brother by Alkaadhem to four winners, dam Flat/hurdle winner

runners.name: Secret Moon
runners.spotlight: Sea Moon mare out of a Flat and hurdles winner; well beaten on only point-to-point start
runners.comment: Sea Moon mare out of a Flat and hurdles winner, well beaten on only point-to-point start

runners.name: No Mon No Fun
runners.spotlight: Fair pedigree; failed to complete two starts in points; Jack Doyle fits him with a tongue-tie for racecourse debut
runners.comment: Fair pedigree, failed to complete two starts in points, tongue-tie on for racecourse debut

runners.name: Rusty Finure
runners.spotlight: Weak bumper form in 2024; no better at Listowel last month after a long absence.
runners.comment: Weak bumper form in 2024, no better at Listowel last month after a long absence

runners.name: Venture Maximus
runners.spotlight: Second foal; Mahler gelding; dam was placed over hurdles; pedigree credentials are weak.
runners.comment: Second foal, Mahler gelding; dam was placed over hurdles; pedigree credentials are weak.

runners.name: Riverstown Boy
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in points after finishing second in a four-horse race on his debut; may be able to build on a fair effort on hurdles debut at Clonmel; will probably need to get a handicap mark.
runners.comment: Ordinary form in points, may be able to build on a fair effort on hurdles debut at Clonmel

runners.name: Becca Sommers
runners.spotlight: By Maxios out of the brilliant Solerina; remote fourth over C&D on debut, can improve
runners.comment: By Maxios out of the brilliant Solerina, remote fourth over C&D on debut, can improve

runners.name: Denham Rock
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential in three stats; his trainer has a leading candidate in Jack Owen Jack.
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential, trainer has a leading candidate in Jack Owen Jack

runners.name: Capri Gear
runners.spotlight: Remote ninth on debut at Ballinrobe; Jack Owen Jack is the stable's main hope here.
runners.comment: Remote ninth on debut at Ballinrobe, Jack Owen Jack is the stable's main hope here

runners.name: Mambo Du Large
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in a point; remote seventh at Clonmel on hurdling debut; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, pulled up in a point, remote seventh at Clonmel on hurdling debut

runners.name: Matcho d'Agrostis
runners.spotlight: Fell on his first two starts over hurdles; never involved at Punchestown; second reserve
runners.comment: Second reserve, has fallen twice, never involved at Punchestown

races.race_name: Joe Walsh Flooring Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Cocovic
runners.spotlight: Point winner; runner up in maiden hurdles at Kilbeggan, Killarney and Sligo last summer; beaten almost 10l in third over C&D on handicap debut five weeks ago; should improve from the run, his first since last autumn.
runners.comment: Point winner, placed in three maiden hurdles, beaten almost 10l in a C&D handicap
runners.owner: Treaty Retail Invest' Holdings Ltd/Denis Murphy B'stock Ltd

runners.name: Bhean Saibhre
runners.spotlight: Had been mixing Flat and hurdling action to useful effect before an off-day at Ballinrobe in May; last year's maiden hurdle win was on good ground.
runners.comment: Had been mixing Flat and hurdling action to useful effect before an off-day at Ballinrobe

runners.name: Cullenwaine
runners.spotlight: Modest strike-rate and both wins were in 2024; has been chasing; 5lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Modest strike-rate and both wins were in 2024, has been chasing, 5lb out of the handicap

runners.name: Warm In Gorey
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner; endured a dismal spell of form in the first half of the year, when pulled up in three consecutive chase starts; signs of a recovery over hurdles recently.
runners.comment: Dismal spell of form in the first half of the year, signs of a recovery recently

runners.name: Qaasid
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner in Britain; promoted winner of C&D handicap in which Friar Hogan was carried out; he had also suffered interference caused by the first-past-the-post; went up 6lb and failed to distinguish himself at Punchestown.
runners.comment: Promoted winner of C&D handicap in which Friar Hogan was carried out, poor run last time
runners.owner: Four Fools & A Fast Horse Syndicate

runners.name: Pure Logic
runners.spotlight: Very solid bumper form, taking the runner-up spot three times between June and August two years ago; placed in a Ballinrobe maiden hurdle last year; more recent hurdles form is much less convincing; may find her level in handicaps; good 5lb claimer booked.
runners.comment: Solid bumper form in 2024, hurdles form is much less convincing, may find her level now

runners.name: Friar Hogan
runners.spotlight: Not far off the pace when carried out at the second-last over C&D on latest, a race awarded to today's rival Qaasid on the disqualification of the offending Marian Avenue; it looked as if he was set to deliver a clear career-best; solid chance with that in mind.
runners.comment: Not far off the pace when carried out at the second-last over C&D on latest, solid chance

runners.name: The First Turn
runners.spotlight: Thurles bumper winner on debut last October; set a couple of stiff tasks over hurdles in recent months; sights lowered now for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, set stiff tasks over hurdles, sights lowered now for handicap debut

runners.name: General Clermont
runners.spotlight: Has been chasing, pulled up on latest; 1-33 hurdles record is a significant negative but he has a decent record in place terms; course winner over fences two seasons ago.
runners.comment: Has been chasing, 1-33 hurdles record is a negative but has a decent record in place terms

runners.name: Mickey Cohen
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m2f beginners' chase at Downpatrick (yielding) last autumn; placed over hurdles on either side of that first success; might need this on first outing after a lengthy break.
runners.comment: Won over fences last autumn, might need this on first outing after a lengthy break

runners.name: Committee Meeting
runners.spotlight: Point winner; some promise in maiden hurdles at Punchestown and Thurles in 2024; has struggled in two handicap hurdles after a spell over fences; stablemate Warm In Gorey is more appealing.
runners.comment: Has struggled in two handicap hurdles after a spell over fences, up against it

runners.name: Desert Cave
runners.spotlight: Moderate overall form since Kilbeggan win two years ago; has changed stable since finishing down the field in a Kilbeggan race in which Bhean Saibhre finished fifth; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Moderate overall form since Kilbeggan win two years ago; has changed stable, hard to fancy

runners.name: Global Skies
runners.spotlight: Won as a 2yo in Britain; failed to make much impression over hurdles last winner; different proposition now; has gone up 13lb for his improved performances (second, followed by a win) at Clonmel this season; sustained progress is feasible
runners.comment: Has gone up 13lb for his improved performances at Clonmel, sustained progress is feasible

runners.name: Moonlight Paradise
runners.spotlight: Point winner, ordinary form in maiden hurdles; should be more effective in handicaps; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Point winner, ordinary form in maiden hurdles, should be more effective in handicaps

runners.name: The Vegas Raider
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner; 1-17 over hurdles; entitled to need this after missing last season.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner, 1-17 over hurdles, entitled to need this after missing last season

races.race_name: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Le Fanu
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 18th attempt at Listowel (2m5f, good to yielding) last time when 2.25l ahead of Illtakehimmyself; up 5lb but he should go well once again.
runners.comment: Off the mark at 18th attempt last time, 2.25l ahead of Illtakehimmyself; up 5lb now

runners.name: Charlie's Dilemma
runners.spotlight: 0-11 over hurdles but placed six times in maidens; soundly beaten in opening handicap run at Musselburgh and similar story back in maiden company at Tramore; tailed off in a Ballinrobe handicap in May (2m6f, good); blinkers go on now but others appeal more.
runners.comment: 0-11 over hurdles and recent outings have no been encouraging; others appeal more

runners.name: Whatafoolbelieves
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in a bumper but not much in maiden hurdles; still held a chance when unseating at two out on handicap debut at Downpatrick (2m6f, good); poor at Sligo last time (2m5f, good).
runners.comment: Still held a chance when UR on h'cap bow; poor at Sligo last time

runners.name: U Asking Me
runners.spotlight: Point winner bagged a sole hurdles victory at Clonmel in 2024 (2m4f, good) off 2lb lower; tailed off over hurdles and pulled up over fences in two runs this year.
runners.comment: Won a h'cap in 2024; tailed off and PU in two runs this year

runners.name: Stretchemout
runners.spotlight: Sligo beginners' chase winner in October over 2m5f on yielding for Gordon Elliott; failed to build on that in three subsequent runs; makes stable debut/seasonal return back over hurdles now.
runners.comment: Beginners' chase winner was poor in final starts for former yard; makes seasonal return

runners.name: Smallcraftwarning
runners.spotlight: First reserve; two hurdling wins and one chase victory; some positive runs over fences, including when third of eight here in May (3m1f, good); never better than midfield back over hurdles at Sligo last time.
runners.comment: Two hurdling wins and one chase victory; moderate return to hurdles last time

runners.name: She'll Be Kept
runners.spotlight: Maiden from eight point starts and 11 under rules; tailed off over 2m4f here in May (soft) on stable debut; others more appealing.
runners.comment: M'den in points and under rules; tailed off here last time; others more appealing

runners.name: Mollys Dolly
runners.spotlight: Winless from 11 starts (eight over hurdles); okay fifth of 12 in a moderate Wexford maiden hurdle (2m4f, good to yielding) in May; 19.5l behind at Listowel in May (2m5f, good to yielding).
runners.comment: Winless from 11; beaten 19.5l in a Listowel h'cap last time

runners.name: Any Chance
runners.spotlight: Nice effort in third maiden hurdle start over 2m here in March when a 5l fifth of 12 (soft); okay sixth at Wexford on handicap bow (2m1f, good to yielding); more needed to play a starring role.
runners.comment: Good fifth in a m'den here and okay sixth on h'cap bow latest; more needed

runners.name: Stumblin In
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m6f Tramore handicap hurdle in April last year (yielding) off 88; tailed off there back from a break in April (2m1f, good to yielding) but better effort on debut for current yard at Clonmel when sixth of 16 (2m4f, good), keeping on powerfully after not travelling early on; not dismissed in first-time tongue tie.
runners.comment: Kept on strongly for sixth last time; not dismissed in first-time tongue tie

runners.name: Illtakehimmyself
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on handicap debut here over 2m3f on soft to heavy ground but has finished runner-up in his two starts this season; beaten a short head at Punchestown (3m ,good to yielding) raised 3lb and was 2.25l behind Le Fanu at Listowel last time (2m5f, good to yielding); he's up 2lb more but his turn is coming.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two h'caps this season' 2.25l behind Le Fanu latest; his turn is coming

runners.name: Ballinaboola Gold
runners.spotlight: Ran her best race in her fourth handicap start at Punchestown in May (3m, good) when runner-up, well clear of the remainder; up 3lb but not at the same level when fifth at Wexford next time (3m, good to yielding); chance based on her Punchestown run.
runners.comment: Good second on penultimate but didn't build on that latest; chance at her best

runners.name: Liss Eile
