runners.spotlight: Pulled up at Tramore in April but then her best handicap effort came back at that track the following month when a 2.5l fourth of 15 (2m, good); didn't build on that at Clonmel when well beaten (2m1f, good); others more appealing.
runners.comment: Good fourth in a Tramore h'cap but didn't build on that at Clonmel; others more appealing

runners.name: Friends Of Barry
runners.spotlight: Didn't show anything in four maiden hurdle runs but much better on handicap bow at Tramore when a clear runner-up in first-time cheekpieces (2m6f, good, 33-1); up 3lb but of definite interest.
runners.comment: Improved markedly on h'cap bow when clear runner-up; of definite interest up 3lb

runners.name: Sallap
runners.spotlight: Won a Clonmel handicap in October (2m7f, yielding) off 73 and was in the lead when falling at Thurles next time; poor on return at Cork but won again back at Clonmel last month (2m7f, good), although dangerous-looking leader fell at two out; up 5lb taking account of rider's claim; in the mix but needs to prove she can do it away from Clonmel.
runners.comment: Two wins came at Clonmel inc' latest; in the mix off 5lb higher considering rider's claim

runners.name: Stone Rulya
runners.spotlight: Ordinary Flat form and tailed off at triple-figure prices in four maiden hurdles; the blinkers are new but hard to make a case for on handicap bow.
runners.comment: Ordinary Flat form and in m'den hurdles; hard to make a case for on h'cap bow

runners.name: Can't Catch Molly
runners.spotlight: Finished midfield in four of her five outings to date, including in her second handicap start here when upped to an extended 2m3f trip (soft to heavy), weakening before two out; up in distance again; has never raced on better than yielding ground.
runners.comment: Midfield finishes in four of five runs inc' over 2m4f here last time; up in distance again

runners.name: Tennesse Boy
runners.spotlight: Yet to trouble the judge in seven runs over hurdles; went close over fences at Sligo in October (3m2f, good to yielding); tailed off at Thurles in October; pulled up back from a break in a Ballingarry point-to-point in May (evens fav'); others preferred.
runners.comment: Yet to trouble the judge in seven hurdle runs; PU when fav' back from break in a point
runners.owner: Exors of the Late David Cotter

runners.name: Ocean Murphy
runners.spotlight: Made late headway in a couple of maidens and upped markedly in trip to 2m6f for his handicap debut at Downpatrick when a never nearer fourth (2m6f, yielding to soft); dropped 1lb and cheekpieces go on but better ground a question.
runners.comment: Never nearer fourth on h'cap bow over 2m6f; dropped 1lb and cheekpieces on

races.race_name: Munster Suite @ Limerick Races Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Phildante
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a 3m handicap at Wexford last summer (good); struggled after that, but her 9l fourth of 15 at Sligo (2m5f, good) last time was more promising; place chance if building on that.
runners.comment: Minor promise in her 9l fourth of 15 last time; place chance if building on it

runners.name: Mickey Hulie
runners.spotlight: Dual-point winner bagged a first success under rules in May when keeping on well to win over C&D off 7lb lower (soft) and the runner-up won next time; has his chance of following up but this better ground and revised mark is a different test.
runners.comment: Got a first success under rules over C&D last time; 7lb higher mark and better ground now

runners.name: Ellies Rock
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Clonmel in April (2m, soft) off 6lb lower; stamina test remains in doubt after moderate fifth at Tramore next time (2m5f, yielding); fell at the last when weakening on chase debut here last time (2m2f, good to yielding); capable of winning but beaten fav' last twice.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Clonmel but modest 5th over further next time; fell on chase debut latest

runners.name: Western Opera
runners.spotlight: 0-10 and was pulled up at Wexford on stable debut in May (2m1f, good to yielding); ran one of her best races at Clonmel over an inadequate extended 2m trip (good) when a 4l fifth, keeping on; this return to further should suit and she has her chance if she can find some consistency.
runners.comment: 0-10 but ran one of her best races over inadequate trip last time; chance with a repeat

runners.name: Ishan
runners.spotlight: Three-time winning hurdler in 2023, showing a liking for a test of stamina in testing conditions; now 7lb below his last winning mark but not much encouragement in his two Clonmel outings for his new stable.
runners.comment: Three-time winning hurdler in 2023; 7lb below last winning mark but recent form poor

runners.name: The Rebel County
runners.spotlight: Hunter chase winner at Tipperary last year has been hit or miss since; one of the hits came at Downpatrick last time when beaten 3.75l in third in a handicap chase (3m, good); reverts to hurdles now off 13lb lower mark but is 0-7 in this sphere without troubling judge.
runners.comment: Hunter chase winner was a decent third over fences last time; reverts to hurdles

runners.name: Ballyredinkingston
runners.spotlight: Showed a bit in bumpers last year; never on terms when keeping on for sixth of 12 finishers at Kilbeggan in June (3m1f, good to yielding); down 1lb and he's well treated based on his early form.
runners.comment: Showed a bit in bumpers last year but moderate over hurdles; well treated on early form

runners.name: Fire Coral
runners.spotlight: Won handicaps at Downpatrick (2m6f) and Down Royal (3m) last August; not as good at Listowel in September; pulled up on return at Clonmel (2m7f, good); needs to come back to her best.
runners.comment: Won two h'caps in Aug'; PU on return; needs to come back to best

runners.name: Set The Tone
runners.spotlight: 0-15 and yet to deliver on promise shown in 3yo hurdles in 2024; badly out of form over fences and hurdles in 2026; not easy to make a case for.
runners.comment: 0-15 and badly out of form in 2026; not easy to make a case for

runners.name: Gortmore Lady
runners.spotlight: Now 0-17 but hit the frame a few times off higher marks for Tony Martin; in midfield when brought down at the second last at Downpatrick (2m6f, good top yielding) in a first-time tongue tie (discarded) on stable debut; may come on for that seasonal return, but needs to.
runners.comment: 0-17 but hit the frame a few times off higher marks; BD when in midfield on stable debut

runners.name: Technology
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden over hurdles; best form has been at Wexford; makes seasonal return after heavy defeats at Navan and Tipperary when last seen.
runners.comment: 16-race m'den over hurdles; makes seasonal return after heavy defeats when last seen

runners.name: Knocknagoran Lady
runners.spotlight: Maiden's best efforts have come on good ground, including over this trip at Down Royal in April when third of 19 (3m, good) in this headgear; below that level but not disgraced in sixth at Wexford next time (3m, good to yielding); place claims.
runners.comment: Best form on good ground inc' when third at Down Royal on penultimate; place claims

runners.name: Hob's Angel
runners.spotlight: 10yo mare opened her account over hurdles at the 16th attempt at Clonmel in May (2m4f, good to yielding); distant second (winner DSQ) over 2m4f (soft) here off this 6lb higher mark last time; this ground suits better and she can continue to acquit herself well.
runners.comment: Opened hurdling account at 16th attempt at Clonmel; distant second off new mark last time

runners.name: Annie Questions
runners.spotlight: Belatedly off the mark in point-to-points in May; once placed over hurdles but that was two years ago; no impact in a handicap chase at Roscommon last month.
runners.comment: Belatedly off the mark in points in May; no impact in a h'cap chase last month

runners.name: Tuff Days
runners.spotlight: Winless from 33 starts under rules; recent efforts over fences here and hurdles at Kilbeggan suggest he's not going to change that today.
runners.comment: 0-33 and recent starts here over fences and hurdles at Kilbeggan are not encouraging

runners.name: Lady Aquarelle
runners.spotlight: Hasn't found much improvement over modest maiden hurdle form in handicaps in her last two runs; beaten 21l here when last seen in May (2m6f, soft); needs to find more.
runners.comment: Hasn't improved for switch to h'caps last twice; needs to find more

runners.name: Another Glendaars
runners.spotlight: First reserve; just 5-1 here in May when returning from a 530-day absence, but tailed off; improved effort at Downpatrick on good ground when a 16l fourth of 13, running on; he's going the right way.
runners.comment: Reserve; tailed off back from long absence here but was better last time

runners.name: Whatyouwant
runners.spotlight: Doubled her hurdles tally at Kilbeggan in September (3m1f, good) off 5lb lower in first-time cheekpieces at 80-1; pulled up at Listowel when last seen later that month (2m6f, good to yielding); hard to trust, especially back from an absence.
runners.comment: Doubled hurdles tally at 80-1 in first-time cheekpieces in Sept'; PU when last seen

runners.name: Captain Kate
runners.spotlight: Runner-up once over hurdles from nine starts (Wexford, 3m, good); sixth in opening two chase starts on better ground but was tailed off over an extended 3m on heavy at Punchestown when last seen in November; improvement required and may need this.
runners.comment: Runner-up once over hurdles from nine starts; improvement required and may need this

races.race_name: INPBA Supporting Irish Racing INH Flat Race (In Memory Of Jerry McDonnell)

runners.name: Bacharach
runners.spotlight: Weakened tamely at Punchestown on bumper debut but much better in an added tongue tie (retained) at Ballinrobe when a clear runner-up (Time For Talking behind in third); Derek O'Connor is back aboard and big run expected.
runners.comment: Clear runner-up on second bumper start in added tongue tie; top rider back aboard

runners.name: Caislean Gear
runners.spotlight: Clear second behind easy Willie Mullins winner on debut at Roscommon (yielding to soft); that run puts him on the shortlist with normal improvement.
runners.comment: Clear second behind easy Mullins winner on debut; that runs puts him on shortlist

runners.name: The President
runners.spotlight: Walk In The Park filly the first foal out of top-class racemare Benie Des Dieux; despite her pedigree, she was relatively unfancied on her debut at Listowel and finished a distant third; needs to show a good bit more.
runners.comment: Out of top-class racemare Benie Des Dieux but bundles of improvement needed over her debut

runners.name: Frisco Lad
runners.spotlight: Walk In The Park gelding; dam maiden in points and 2m-2m4f hurdles, closely related to winners Offaly (bumper/2m4f hurdle/2m5f chase) and Blencathra Bay (bumper); top rider aboard and in good hands so worth a market check.
runners.comment: Walk In The Park gelding; top rider aboard and in good hands; check market
runners.owner: A Walk In The Park Syndicate

runners.name: Time For Talking
runners.spotlight: Did well on debut to take third at Ballinrobe, particularly as his rider lost his balance and irons after a few furlongs before regaining them; 3.5l behind Bacharach on that occasion and 5lb worse off now so work to do.
runners.comment: Did well on debut to take third but was 3.5l behind Bacharach and is 5lb worse off now

runners.name: Maggies Yard
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Henschke (2m5f/3m1f chase), Reserve Judgement (2m7f/3m1f hurdle) and Nanny Betty (point); dam unraced half-sister to 2m5f-3m hurdle/chase winner Letbeso, family of high-class staying chasers Grey Abbey and The Grey Monk; trainer 0-33 in bumpers last five seasons.
runners.comment: Half-sister to three winners; trainer 0-33 in bumpers last five seasons

runners.name: River Cartwright
runners.spotlight: 16,000euros 3yo; seventh foal; half-brother to bumper/2m7f hurdle winner Chance The Robin; unraced dam closely related to high-class bumper and 2m-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Snoopy Loopy, half-sister to useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Sam Adams; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Half-brother to bumper/2m7f hurdle winner Chance The Robin; others more appealing

runners.name: Village Star
runners.spotlight: Brother to 2m4f hurdle winner Parish Star, half-brother to Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner Eastern Voice (RPR 90); dam maiden (61) sister to 6f 2yo/2m hurdle winner Freedom Square, half-sister to useful 1m4f Flat/2m hurdle winner Via Galilei; best watched.
runners.comment: Brother to 2m4f hurdle winner Parish Star (RPR 85); best watched

runners.name: Mountain Sky
runners.spotlight: Fine Flat pedigree; sold by Godolphin unraced for 40,000gns last autumn; 11-1 for bumper debut at Killarney (2m1f, good yielding) but raced wide and was well beaten; much more required.
runners.comment: Fine Flat pedigree was sold by Godolphin unraced for 40,000gns; well beaten on debut

races.race_name: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Cashel Queen
runners.spotlight: Stuck to her task well when landing Naas barrier trial in May; solid debut effort at Leopardstown (7f, soft) when beaten 5l, finishing ahead of Celtic Moon, Cloughleagh Bridge and Caoch The Piper; sets the standard and the promise of better ground should suit.
runners.comment: Barrier trial winner; in front of 3 of today's rivals on debut; sets the standard

runners.name: Celtic Moon
runners.spotlight: Thunder Moon filly caught the eye when beaten 0.25l behind Cashel Queen in fourth on debut at Leopardstown three weeks ago; stable among the winners and improvement is likely; in the mix.
runners.comment: Not much to separate her and Cashel Queen at Leopardstown; right in the mix

runners.name: Party In Vegas
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; 35,000gns 2yo; half-sister to Italian 7.5f/1m winner; dam 7f AW winner, out of half-sister to winners Tipsy Creek (5f Group 2), Abunawwas (7f Group 3) and Magic Cove (1m Listed); stable won three of last six runnings of this; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Sixth foal; 35,000gns 2yo; stable won three of last six runnings of this; shortlisted

runners.name: Ippy
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal cost 20,000euros as yearling; half-sister to winners Lady Of Inishfree (7f 2yo; RPR 94) and Chukotka (KSA 1m2f); dam 1m2f turf/1m4f AW winner (83), out of 12.5f Group 2 winner; stable 0-4 with 2yos this year; hooded.
runners.comment: Sixth foal cost 20,000euros as yearling; stable 0-4 with 2yos this year; hooded
runners.owner: Paul J Nolan/Walerc Limited/M J Wasylocha/Tony Cantwell

runners.name: Violet Be Humble
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam placed 6f 2yo, half-sister to winners Oh Purple Reign (Listed-placed 6f-1m) and Warsaw Road (7f); dam placed 6f 2yo (70); stable 0-39 with 2yos; best watched.
runners.comment: Dam placed 6f 2yo, half-sister to Oh Purple Reign (Listed-placed); stable 0-39 with 2yos

runners.name: Sister Nancy
runners.spotlight: Slowly away on 6.5f debut at Listowel but it was a respectable fourth behind the winner Beibhinn who finished third in a Group 2 subsequently, while runner-up Big Negotiator won at York before midfield in the Queen Mary; could improve up in trip.
runners.comment: Slowly away on debut but respectable run in a race that has worked out well; chance

runners.name: Cloughleagh Bridge
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal cost 37,000euros; looked in need of the experience when fifth of eight on 7f Leopardstown debut three weeks ago, weakening late on; behind Cashel Queen and Celtic Moon on that occasion so needs to improve.
runners.comment: Was a good few lengths behind Cashel Queen and Celtic Moon on debut; improvement needed

runners.name: Lucy's Promise
runners.spotlight: Cheap purchase was well beaten in Naas barrier trial but better than bare result on 6f debut at Fairyhouse, weakening late on after forceful tactics early; needs to find more but hood may help.
runners.comment: Better than bare result on debut at Fairyhouse, weakening late on after forceful tactics

runners.name: Caoch The Piper
runners.spotlight: Behind some of these on debut at Leopardstown and poor effort at Down Royal; work to do.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two maidens at Leopardstown and Down Royal

races.race_name: Champion Lettings Handicap

runners.name: Borora Aura
runners.spotlight: Third placing in a Ballinrobe claimer was a step in the right direction; not without her backers in first two handicap outings but has failed to deliver; tongue-tie is now reached for.
runners.comment: Not without her backers in first 2 handicap outings but has failed to deliver; tongue-tie

runners.name: Bullet Bourbon
runners.spotlight: Second runner for local yard; maiden went closest when chasing home a progressive type over 6f at Dundalk in April; has a tendency to miss the break; short of room and hampered 2f out when behind third-placed Fontaine Khaled at Down Royal on latest; not discounted.
runners.comment: Hampered 2f out when behind Fontaine Khaled at Down Royal on latest; not discounted
runners.owner: David Daly & Gerard Shevlin & D Battersby

runners.name: Caoilte
runners.spotlight: First five runs this year on the AW, best effort on 7f handicap debut when beaten around 5l into fifth; finished last next time and no better on turf debut at Fairyhouse in May; new headgear combination; others preferred.
runners.comment: Only one okay effort on the AW, the rest has been very poor; others preferred

runners.name: Beau Army
runners.spotlight: Has won over 6f/7f at Dundalk; ran her best race on turf when fourth behind the progressive Meriden at Down Royal on penultimate (one place behind Fontaine Khaled); nowhere near that level at Limerick since but player if at her best.
runners.comment: Multiple AW winner; right behind Fontaine Khaled on penultimate; poor at Limerick

runners.name: Enthusiastically
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Dundalk nursery on AW debut last November when trained by Donnacha O'Brien; didn't quite see out the stiff mile when close fourth at Dundalk on yard debut; completely tailed off over 6f at the Curragh on latest; headgear combination is back on eased in grade.
runners.comment: Won 7f AW nursery for Donnacha O'Brien; promise on yard debut but tailed off on latest

runners.name: Fontaine Khaled
runners.spotlight: Well held first two handicap starts but not beaten far at Naas in April and solid third of 14 at Down Royal (7f, good) on latest; the winner Meriden has gone on to win three more on the bounce (now rated 97) and three further winners have come out of the race; shortlisted up in trip.
runners.comment: The form of her latest third at Down Royal has worked out extremely well; shortlisted
runners.owner: Dan Kiely & C O'Neill & Apollo Thoroughbreds

runners.name: Wedding Year
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; weak turf form last season; maiden's best form is on the AW; has plenty to find judged on two latest efforts at Gowran and Ballinrobe; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Has plenty to find judged on two latest efforts at Gowran and Ballinrobe; new headgear

runners.name: Due In June
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in three 7f maidens; failed to land a blow on 6f Fairyhouse handicap debut last month; Colin Keane booked stepping up in trip; watch the market for clues.
runners.comment: No joy yet; Colin Keane booked stepping up in trip; watch the market for clues

runners.name: Wicklow Gap
runners.spotlight: Penultimate Roscommon run his best yet, good fourth after not clear run in first-time tongue-tie; wide draw and testing ground valid excuses for poor effort at Leopardstown; better run expected here.
runners.comment: Solid chance judged on penultimate Roscommon run; excuses lto; better chance here

runners.name: Cause I Like You
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; never better than fourth in 12 outings; not a bad effort when fifth of 14 at Ballinrobe (1m2f) ten days ago but will need to pull out more to win this.
runners.comment: Second runner for yard; never better than fourth in 12 outings; needs to pull out more

runners.name: Sayonara
runners.spotlight: Reserve; some decent runs in defeat on AW over 5f-6f during the winter but has failed to beat a single rival home in two turf starts this year since being gelded; first try beyond 6f.
runners.comment: Has failed to beat a rival home in two turf starts this year; first try beyond 6f; reserve

runners.name: Threebiggulps
runners.spotlight: Weak form in maidens; given a positive ride but ultimately faded out of things on Gowran handicap debut (9.5f); cheekpieces; others look more likely.
runners.comment: Given a positive ride but ultimately faded out of things on Gowran handicap debut (9.5f)

runners.name: Stonyfalls
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in maidens; 66-1 for handicap debut when sixth of 14 at Down Royal (7f, good), not far behind Fontaine Khaled and Beau Army; failed to build on that at that venue on soft 13 days ago but could bounce back if the ground is good.
runners.comment: Not far behind Fontaine Khaled and Beau Army on penultimate; soft an excuse last time

runners.name: Nosmokewithoutfire
runners.spotlight: Out the back in three maidens over trips ranging from 7f-1m4f; handicaps have likely always been the plan so worth a market check for local trainer.
runners.comment: Handicaps has likely always been the plan so worth a market check for local trainer
runners.owner: Ms Denise McCourt & Stephen Sweeney & Maire Bailey

runners.name: Dream Legend
runners.spotlight: Down the field in turf/AW contests last year; long-priced C&D winner (soft) in April on first attempt at the trip before solid third at Gowran (7f, soft) from 9lb higher; would probably want rain but holds every chance.
runners.comment: Long-priced C&D winner in April; good third at Gowran from 9lb higher; probably wants rain
runners.owner: E D Delany & Mrs Lisa Delany

runners.name: Hugo Spritz
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure prices for all three maiden runs when well beaten; cheekpieces on for handicap debut at a more realistic level now handicapping; market will reveal expectations.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces on for handicap debut at a more realistic level now handicapping

races.race_name: Ploughman Bar And Grill Handicap

runners.name: Desert Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: Fourth runner for local yard; still a maiden after 18 starts, placed four times; had a good spell on AW early in the year; market can separate stable's runners.
runners.comment: Fourth runner for local yard; still a maiden after 18 starts, placed four times

runners.name: Shelbourne Flyer
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; maiden after 14 starts; heavily supported in the betting when sixth of 14 at Roscommon (7.5f, yielding) on yard debut last month.
runners.comment: Heavily supported in betting when sixth at Roscommon (7.5f) on yard debut; reserve

runners.name: Dinamine
runners.spotlight: Dual winner, including over C&D (good to yielding) last August off 3lb lower; best effort in three runs this year when close third at Limerick (7f, yielding) last month; slow break costly at that track a day later; could bounce back.
runners.comment: C&D win last year; eyecatching effort at Limerick on penultimate; slow break lto; chance

runners.name: Rockbury Lad
runners.spotlight: Sole run at this track resulted in C&D win (good) in April 2025 off 9lb higher; posted a couple of good efforts on the AW early this year; well held next two starts at Leopardstown; step in the right direction there last time when sixth of 14 after a slow break.
runners.comment: Sole run at the track resulted in C&D win off 9lb higher; step in right direction lto
runners.owner: P E Ryan & J G Shanahan

runners.name: Mullacash Buzz
runners.spotlight: First reserve; four-time winner on AW has scored twice on turf; weakened into sixth at Roscommon last time.
runners.comment: Four-time winner on AW, twice on turf; weakened into sixth at Roscommon last time; reserve

runners.name: Roman Harry
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner is 0-27 on turf; middling effort at the Curragh last time and overall record on this surface is concerning.
runners.comment: Middling effort at the Curragh last time and overall record on this surface is concerning

runners.name: Micasso
runners.spotlight: No wins in 11 but only his second run in this headgear combination when bang there at Cork (1m, yielding) last September; something amiss next time under Colin Keane at Dundalk; has been off the track since.
runners.comment: Bang there at Cork in September but something amiss next time; returns from break

runners.name: Wellwhatshappenin
runners.spotlight: No wins in six runs for former yard; made an immediate impact for this team with 7f Dundalk win last October; sent off favourite for seasonal reappearance over that C&D in March but never figured; needs to prove his effectiveness on turf.
runners.comment: Best form on the AW, winning 7f handicap last October; needs to prove liking for turf

runners.name: Virgil Tibbs
runners.spotlight: Shaped quite well at Galway last August on third maiden start; not a great deal of promise in handicaps, no better than seventh in four attempts; returns from 239-day absence.
runners.comment: Not a great deal of promise in handicaps, no better than seventh in four attempts

runners.name: Pinball Wizard
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; six-time winner in Britain; only glimpses of peak form since second in a claimer early last season, including third over C&D off 9lb higher; seventh of 16 over 1m at Gowran on seasonal debut; place chance.
runners.comment: Six-time winner in Britain; chance judged on C&D third last season from 9lb higher

runners.name: Carvalhal
runners.spotlight: Winner over 1m1f on quick ground in Britain; built on Limerick second last month when scoring decisively at that venue (1m, soft) nine days later; up 6lb but no reason why he can't go well again.
runners.comment: Built on Limerick second last month when scoring decisively at that venue (1m, soft)

runners.name: Dynamic Force
runners.spotlight: Four wins at up to 1m but 0-19 for this yard; good effort despite racing keenly when close fourth of 14 at Down Royal (7f, good) on penultimate; dwelt over 6f at Fairyhouse on latest; worth a market check for local handler with Colin Keane booked.
runners.comment: Good 7f fourth at Down Royal; dwelt over 6f at Fairyhouse; watch market for local yard

runners.name: Captain Ciano
runners.spotlight: Second runner for local yard; best run was at an early stage of last season; a long way below that standard this year; leap of faith required.
runners.comment: Best run was at an early stage of last season; a long way below that standard this year

runners.name: Tortola
runners.spotlight: Weak form for Josh Halley; best run for Andy Slattery on yard debut when third in 7f claimer at Dundalk; regressive since; first run for Eoin Griffin; best watched.
runners.comment: Has shown very limited form for two trainers; first run for Eoin Griffin now

runners.name: Hastily
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; three-time turf winner, the latest at Cork last July (6f, good to firm) and good C&D second in August; shown very little since.
runners.comment: Won at Cork last July (6f) and good C&D second in August; shown very little since; reserve

runners.name: Secret Magician
runners.spotlight: Last turf win came at Naas in August 2024 (7f, good); signs he could capitalise on sliding mark when close third at Killarney (1m, good) in May but disappointing favourite at Listowel next time; failed to see out 1m1f on soft at Leopardstown on latest but definite player at his best.
runners.comment: Failed to see out 1m1f on soft at Leopardstown on latest but definite player at his best

runners.name: Rampage
runners.spotlight: Third runner for local yard; four-time winner; last turf win in 2023 over 8.5f at Galway (soft); two AW wins since; hard to fancy on this year's form but has placed multiple times over C&D, including when runner-up at this festival last year from a stone higher; can't dismiss.
runners.comment: Hard to fancy on this year's form but 2nd at this festival last year from a stone higher
runners.owner: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Syndicate/S Sweeney

runners.name: Plastic Paddy
runners.spotlight: Seven-time AW winner in Britain for Mick Appleby; no success since 2024; has shown nothing in 15 outings on the Flat and over hurdles for current yard.
runners.comment: Has shown nothing in 15 outings on the Flat and over hurdles for current yard

races.race_name: Irish EBF Auction Series Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Bit Of A Buzz
runners.spotlight: Sets a solid standard judged on his four maiden efforts to date, with marginally the best effort at Fairyhouse on penultimate (6f, good); not as good dropped to this trip at Down Royal (soft) 13 days ago but likely to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Sets a solid standard judged on his four maiden efforts to date; on the premises again

runners.name: Hip Hop Chrissy
runners.spotlight: Second in Naas barrier trial in May; made a satisfactory debut five days later when third at Listowel (6.5f, good; the winner and runner-up ran at Royal Ascot subsequently); this opposition looks less demanding and she should have the speed for this trip.
runners.comment: Second in barrier trial; third in a decent Listowel maiden; this is less demanding
runners.owner: Eclipse T'bred Partners/Gaynor Bloodstock LLC/F Gallagher

runners.name: The Drambling Man
runners.spotlight: Hung right when not beaten far behind Bit Of A Buzz on 5f Cork debut; well beaten behind Hop Hop Chrissy when upped to 6.5f at Listowel; debut effort offers some hope.
runners.comment: Enters this judged on Cork debut effort; nowhere near as good at Listowel

runners.name: Annie Batt
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; 2,500GBP yearling; half-sister to winners Jer Batt (5f inc AW; RPR 100) and Buddy Batt (5f inc 2yo/AW); dam 5f winner, half-sister to winners Another Batt (Turkish 6f 2yo Listed) and Iva Batt (5f/6f); market can guide.
runners.comment: 2,500GBP yearling; half-sister to Jer Batt (5f inc AW; RPR 100); market can guide

runners.name: Razmataz
runners.spotlight: Cheap purchase; third in Naas barrier trial in May; slowly into stride and never a factor on racecourse debut at Fairyhouse (6f) last month; stable also runs Hip Hop Chrissy who holds stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Third in barrier trial; slowly away and never a factor on debut

runners.name: Eastern Queen
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; 4,000euros yearling; sister to winners Marty Hopkirk (5f AW; RPR 91) and Commander Straker (5f 2yo; RPR 88), half-sister to Nuevo Slovo (5f 2yo); dam 7f/1m AW winner (81), half-sister to 6f Listed winner Katia; best watched.
runners.comment: Sixth foal; 4,000euros yearling; sister to Marty Hopkirk (5f AW; RPR 91); watch

runners.name: Grayrobin
runners.spotlight: Resold £36,000 yearling who didn't show much on sole start for Archie Watson at Nottingham (6f, good) four weeks ago; market will guide on Irish/stable debut.
runners.comment: Didn't show much on sole start for Archie Watson at Nottingham 4 weeks ago; yard debut

runners.name: Santo Parvenue
runners.spotlight: Not a bad effort on 5f debut at the Curragh when remote fourth, green before making modest late gains; ran to a similar level next time at Cork; perhaps may improve on better ground but will need to.
runners.comment: Two okay 5f efforts in the mud; perhaps can improve on better ground but will need to

runners.name: Ballymackney
runners.spotlight: Earthlight filly cost 20,000euros; wasn't far off Santo Parvenue when seventh of 10 on 5f Cork debut in May; likely improver; worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: Wasn't far off Santo Parvenue when seventh of 10 on 5f Cork debut in May; likely improver

races.race_name: McNallys Pharmacy Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Desert Friend
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose veteran; winner of nine races across both codes; bolted up at Sligo in May (1m6f, yld-sft) off 6lb lower; struggled the last twice however, and previous runs here have not been his best.
runners.comment: Struggled twice since emphatic Sligo win in May, previous runs here not his best

runners.name: Kevin The Great
runners.spotlight: Much-improved effort on debut for this stable at Navan (1m5f, good) 25 days ago when keeping on for a 1.25l fourth of 16; should go well again off 1lb higher if in the same mood.
runners.comment: Much-improved effort on debut for this stable at Navan, go well again off 1lb higher

runners.name: Celestially
runners.spotlight: Maiden hasn't run that badly in two of her three starts in handicaps; kept on well enough over 1m4f at Limerick on latest; dam closely related to 2m winner so may appreciate this longer trip but needs more to get her head in front.
runners.comment: Kept on over 1m4f at Limerick latest, may improve for this longer trip, needs more to win

runners.name: Maxwell Smart
runners.spotlight: Just 1-21 on the Flat but not beaten far the last twice; closely matched with Kevin The Great on Navan running earlier this month; didn't quite see out 2m2f at Down Royal latest; goes well here and big chance back down in trip off last winning mark for local yard.
runners.comment: Knocking on the door, didn't quite see out 2m2f latest, big chance back down in trip
runners.owner: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Synd'/D O'Leary/J McNally

runners.name: Mystic Rose
runners.spotlight: Maiden has shown glimmers, including when a 5.25l fifth at Navan on latest (1m5f, good); weakened late having led that day so still has stamina to prove in first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Weakened late having led at Navan last time, stamina to prove, tongue-tie goes on

runners.name: Velvet And Vine
runners.spotlight: A couple of her runs in 2024 suggested she could win a race but missed almost all of 2025 and heavy defeat on return from long layoff at Down Royal last month; cheekpieces go on and she's on a dangerous mark on her best form so check the market.
runners.comment: Well handicapped based on best form in 2024, may come on for latest, check the market

runners.name: Jurality
runners.spotlight: Gained reward for a string of solid efforts when always well positioned and winning at Navan (1m5f, good) 25 days ago; 6lb higher now but rider's 2lb claim partly negates that; consistent sort is a big player again.
runners.comment: Up 6lb for deserved Navan win, rider takes off handy 2lb, consistent, big player again

runners.name: Miners Cabin
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo didn't show a whole lot in maidens and similar story in first two handicaps; always behind when beating just one rival home at Down Royal latest (2m, good); hard to fancy for now.
runners.comment: Modest form in maidens and in first two handicaps, hard to make a case for on form

runners.name: Voice Of Northeast
runners.spotlight: Best run yet for this yard on seasonal return at Leopardstown where a 2.5l fourth of 19 (1m5f, good); same mark here and should strip fitter now so enters calculations.
runners.comment: Best run yet for this yard on latest, same mark here, strip fitter, enters calculations

runners.name: Bang Po
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since 2023 and was struggling for form but took a step in the right direction when not far behind a couple of these at Navan last time; best form on easy ground, however, so could do with some rain.
runners.comment: On long losing run, better effort at Navan latest, best form on easier ground than this

runners.name: Numidia
runners.spotlight: Four wins on the Flat but hasn't won on turf since June 2024; 4lb below that last success but recent form hasn't indicated that a return to winning ways is imminent; others preferred.
runners.comment: No win on turf since June 2024, recent form modest, others arrive in better form

runners.name: Al What
runners.spotlight: Maiden ran well when a 3.75l fourth of 16 at Roscommon in May for this rider but hasn't backed that up; beaten 15l when down the field at Fairyhouse (1m4f, good) on latest.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on encouraging Roscommon fourth, beaten 15l at Fairyhouse on latest

races.race_name: Seamus Mulvaney Bookmaker, Crockafotha (Q.R.) Race

runners.name: Lark In The Mornin
runners.spotlight: Useful jumper made a bright start on the Flat, winning this race at skinny odds last July then following up in another Q.R. race over 1m6f at Listowel; soundly beaten in Curragh handicap on seasonal return latest but this is easier and he's best treated by race conditions; obvious chance.
runners.comment: Useful jumper, rated 92 on Flat, come on for latest, won this last year, the one to beat

runners.name: The Mediator
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose sort has been running well on the Flat; two solid efforts in Curragh handicaps to sign off last season; okay effort in Q.R. race at Listowel in May and advertised wellbeing with good second in Curragh charity race last week; player.
runners.comment: Okay run at Listowel on seasonal return, good second in charity race latest, player
runners.owner: Mrs Sheila O'Ryan & John F Doyle & Grant Leon

runners.name: Weston
runners.spotlight: Two-time Flat winner when trained in France; has run a couple of decent races for this yard and one of his better efforts came when beaten just 2.5l by Lark In The Mornin when third in this last year; 10lb better off now and should strip fitter for his okay comeback run at Navan; chance.
runners.comment: Beaten 2.5l in this last year, 10lb better off with Lark In The Mornin now, chance
runners.owner: Thomas Cox/Nap Racing Syndicate/James M Nolan

runners.name: Can Happen
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over hurdles at Ballinrobe (2m, good) on debut for this yard in late May off a mark of 105; now makes Flat debut and probably up against it on these terms.
runners.comment: Ballinrobe hurdles win off 105 on latest, makes Flat debut now, probably up against it
runners.owner: Michael J Mulligan & Patrick G Walsh

runners.name: Intense Approach
runners.spotlight: Talented over jumps; rated 137 over hurdles and a five-time winner in that sphere; has struggled in chases on last few starts and has been off since March but returns with local yard among the winners and he bolted up in a bumper here back in 2023; check the market.
runners.comment: 137-rated hurdler, course bumper winner, recent chase form poor but worth market check

runners.name: Talking Tough
runners.spotlight: Rated just 34 on the Flat and no win of any description since 2020; up against it.
runners.comment: Rated 34, no win of any description since 2020, up against it here

runners.name: Swelltime
runners.spotlight: A winner of six races (four over hurdles and two on the level); however, a Flat rating of just 40 leaves him with heaps to find on these unfavourable terms.
runners.comment: Six-time winner, rated just 40 on the Flat, heaps to find on these unfavourable terms

races.race_name: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Khaleejy
runners.spotlight: Won novice at Newcastle (7f, AW) in April on belated debut and better form when close fourth in C&D novice five weeks ago, both under 7lb claimer; plenty in this field have a form chance and he is one of them.
runners.comment: Won novice at Newcastle (7f, AW) in April and close fourth in C&D novice five weeks ago

runners.name: Veil Of Clouds
runners.spotlight: 5-4 favourite when only fifth of six on debut at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm) four weeks ago; always-prominent second of 11 at Southwell (6f, AW) two weeks later, however, and that makes her a contender.
runners.comment: Always-prominent second of 11 at Southwell (6f, AW) two weeks ago makes her a contender

runners.name: Rajiba
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far on debut at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) last November and reappeared with good second in slowly run race there (8.6f, AW) one month ago; refused to enter stalls at Salisbury (7f) three weeks ago; if there's no repeat of that, she's one of the several leading form contenders.
runners.comment: 2nd in slowly run Wolverhampton race (8.6f, AW); refused to enter stalls three weeks ago

runners.name: Kenergy
runners.spotlight: Plenty of ability when taking minor honours at this track in a 7f novice in March and 1m maiden (close from the front) in April; in the mix.
runners.comment: Plenty of ability here in 7f novice in March and 1m maiden (close from the front) in April

runners.name: Shadow Brigade
runners.spotlight: Close third over C&D last August, which requires some respect even though it came in between two heavy defeats (back here latest); gelded since; market can guide for his first run in 300 days.
runners.comment: Close third over C&D last August in between two heavy defeats (here latest); gelded since

runners.name: Gift Box
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns foal by Sergei Prokofiev; first foal; dam 7f-8.6f winner (including AW; RPR 78), half-sister to fairly useful winners Fiscal Policy (5f/6f) and Dittander (5f 2yo), out of German 1,000 Guineas winner; probably up against it on debut.
runners.comment: 10,000gns foal by Sergei Prokofiev; first foal; dam 7f-8.6f winner (including AW; RPR 78)

runners.name: Stormy Music
runners.spotlight: Failed to justify market prominence at Newbury (6f, good) last August on sole run as 2yo; a bit better there (7f, good) on May return but weakened into eighth of 15 and others in this line-up have accomplished more.
runners.comment: Weakened into eighth of 15 at Newbury (7f, good) in May and others have accomplished more

runners.name: Charlie's Cannon
runners.spotlight: 110,000gns yearling by New Bay; fifth foal; half-brother to winners Harold Shand (7f 2yo; RPR 85) and Unpresuming (Hong Kong 7f; 71); dam unraced half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 1 winner Toormore and 7f 2yo Group 2 winner Estidhkaar; hooded newcomer from a leading yard and the market should guide.
runners.comment: 110,000gns yearling by New Bay; hooded newcomer from a leading yard

runners.name: Landslide
runners.spotlight: Two runs on turf, finishing a lot closer at Brighton (6f, good to firm; made most) last time but he was only fifth of six and it was modest form; the equipment he wore that day (hood and cheekpieces) is dispensed with.
runners.comment: A lot closer at Brighton (6f, good to firm; made most) last time but in only fifth of six

runners.name: Screen Actor
runners.spotlight: $100,000 yearling, 40,000gns breeze-up 2yo; refused to enter stalls on intended debut in May; 28-1, slowly away and always behind at Windsor (1m, good to soft) just over three weeks ago; pedigree gives hope for AW surface.
runners.comment: 28-1, slowly away and always behind at Windsor (1m, good to soft) in June

races.race_name: Unibet Supporting Safer Gambling Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Korbut
runners.spotlight: Not far away on last three starts, particularly when second at Southwell (7f, AW; set pace) and Catterick (6f, good to soft; beaten a neck) three weeks ago; one of the form picks and another bold show should be on its way.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck last time; one of the form picks and another bold show should be on its way

runners.name: Gold Star Gazing
runners.spotlight: Third twice over C&D last autumn and same again when close up at Southwell (6f, AW) on reappearance; best form was on second start (before wind surgery) and he kept on stoutly last time, suggesting a return to 7f would suit; gelded since; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Third on all three starts; gelded since; return to 7f can suit; firmly in calculations

runners.name: Yazoo
runners.spotlight: Speedy pedigree but no chance in novice at Yarmouth (6f, soft; 16-1) three weeks ago after badly blowing the start; hooded this time.
runners.comment: 16-1 at Yarmouth (6f, soft; 16-1) three weeks ago but badly blew the start; now hooded

runners.name: Conspiracist
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to winners Sajir (5.5f-7f including 2yo/AW/Group 1; RPR 121) and First Kingdom (1m including AW; 92); about 13l sixth of ten in C&D novice (12-1) five weeks ago was modest form on debut, fading, but he could be better with that under his belt.
runners.comment: About 13l sixth of ten in C&D novice (12-1) five weeks ago was modest form, fading

runners.name: One Of The Boys
runners.spotlight: 9-1, finished 9l behind the pretty useful winner in maiden at Lingfield (7f, AW) in November but beat the other eight; gelded this May; could be a marked improver.
runners.comment: 2nd of ten at Lingfield (7f, AW) in November; gelded this May; could be a marked improver

runners.name: Le Samourai
runners.spotlight: 450,000gns yearling; New Bay half-brother to five winners including Romantic Warrior (6f-1m2f including multiple Group/Grade 1s); slowly away when always behind in novice at Salisbury (7f, soft) three weeks ago but he was the 5-2 favourite, so he's presumably thought capable of much better; tongue tied this time and it will be interesting to see if his backers return.
runners.comment: 450,000gns buy; slowly away and always behind at Salisbury (7f, soft) but he was favourite

runners.name: Cortado Girl
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure odds when showing low-level form at Lingfield in September (7f, AW) and two weeks ago (6f, good); hooded in the latter, having refused to enter stalls at Nottingham (300-1) in April; no claims yet.
runners.comment: Triple-figure odds, twice showed low-level form; refused to enter stalls once in between

runners.name: Brave New World
runners.spotlight: 220,000gns yearling; late foal; brother to two useful 7f winners, but this 3yo was beaten 18l over C&D (33-1) in April and just over 10l at Doncaster (7f, good to firm; 22-1) in early June; modest form.
runners.comment: 220,000gns yearling; modest form after runs over C&D in April and Doncaster 7f in June

runners.name: Lady Fizz
runners.spotlight: 125-1 and hooded, slow-starting last of eight in maiden at Newmarket (1m, good) in April when trained by Des Donovan.
runners.comment: 125-1 and hooded, slow-starting last at Newmarket (1m, good) in April for Des Donovan

races.race_name: Bet £20 Get £20 With Unibet Nursery Handicap

runners.name: Bayside
runners.spotlight: Won a Beverley novice (5f, good to soft) on her second start but she's finished down the field in both subsequent runs; had tough task in the Chesham at Royal Ascot but she's untried on AW and this looks competitive on her nursery debut; others preferred.
runners.comment: Had tough task in the Chesham last time but others are more persuasive
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Civ & B R Millman

runners.name: Time Saxon Warrior
runners.spotlight: Ran green on Bath debut in May but he's shown clear promise with his placed efforts at Windsor (6f, good to firm) and Leicester (7f, good) in last two runs; gave it a good shot from the front when runner-up last time and he's an interesting contender on nursery debut.
runners.comment: Placed in last two runs and his latest second was over 7f; interesting on nursery debut

runners.name: Graceful George
runners.spotlight: No progress in maiden/novice events but he did show promise with his debut fourth here (6f) in April; half-brother to a 2yo winner over this trip and he needs watching in market on nursery debut.
runners.comment: No progress so far but he's a half-brother to a 2yo winner over this trip; watch market

runners.name: Rhodes Runner
runners.spotlight: 75,000gns yearling; hasn't lived up to market expectations in two turf runs but her latest third of six at Doncaster (6.5f, good to firm) was a step forward from her debut effort; should continue to improve with time and distance and she's in the mix on nursery debut; stablemate of Indian Land.
runners.comment: Hasn't lived up to market expectations in two turf runs; now makes nursery debut

runners.name: Bin Waary
runners.spotlight: Encouraging fourth on debut here (6f) in May but he's taken two backward steps since and was tailed off at Leicester (7f, good) last month; still early days but he weakened quickly last time and has something to prove on nursery debut.
runners.comment: Early days but he was tailed off last time and has something to prove on nursery debut

runners.name: Agnes Hathaway
runners.spotlight: Oscar Performance filly; ran green on her debut at Wolverhampton (6f) in May but she's finished in rear again in both subsequent runs; now steps up to 7f on nursery debut but she needs plenty of improvement.
runners.comment: Finished in rear in all three runs and needs plenty of improvement on nursery debut
runners.owner: D Kilburn, C Wright, F Mahony & Partner

runners.name: Magical Life
runners.spotlight: Struggled in her three qualifying runs (5f), with a best RPR of 34; this new trip should be a better fit on pedigree but she's 9lb out of the weights on her nursery debut; passed over.
runners.comment: Struggled in her qualifying runs and is 9lb out of the weights on nursery debut

runners.name: Indian Land
runners.spotlight: Beat only one rival on his Leicester debut but he left that form a long way behind when scoring at Wolverhampton (6f, AW); that form is hard to weigh up but he finished well for a last-gasp success and is open to more progress on this step up to 7f; needs a close look in his first nursery.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in May and he's open to more progress upped to 7f on nursery debut

races.race_name: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div I)

runners.name: Grizedale
runners.spotlight: C&D win last summer on his second start; similar form in defeat on his next two starts but he ended 2025 with a lesser effort and there was no revival on his reappearance at York (1m1f, good; first run since gelded; well backed) 19 days ago; dropped another 3lb and could benefit from the return to AW; still of interest.
runners.comment: C&D winner; too free when well backed at York on return; still has potential back on AW

runners.name: Maximising
runners.spotlight: Ready 7f AW win in January; upped to 1m for the first time at Lingfield in March and produced career-best form, finishing off best behind a winner who was red-hot at the time; absent since but could have more to come at this trip.
runners.comment: 7f AW win in January and ran a fine race upped to 1m when last seen in March; contender

runners.name: Bold Suitor
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; ran creditably here three weeks ago but it was a lower grade and his finishing effort lacked oomph; likely vulnerable at this level.
runners.comment: C&D winner but finishing effort three weeks ago didn't mark him down as the answer here

runners.name: Port Road
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner, two of them over C&D; not at his best here six weeks ago (7f) and a career best will be required to come out on top.
runners.comment: Conditions to suit but his latest eighth here (7f) needs bettering

runners.name: Shihoku
runners.spotlight: Flattered to deceive for James Owen and he was a well-beaten 25-1 shot on his stable debut (1m, good to firm) two weeks ago; effective on AW but others are safer.
runners.comment: Well-beaten 25-1 shot on recent stable debut; too much to prove for comfort

runners.name: Zabeel Alkabeir
runners.spotlight: Easy win at Lingfield (7f, good) on debut before pitched into Group 2 company for his second start; unsurprisingly came up short there but he was quickly back on track when defying a penalty at Southwell (7f) last October; bred to stay 1m and there should be more to come this year.
runners.comment: Two 7f wins sandwich a heavy defeat in Group 2 company; 1m should suit; unexposed

runners.name: Blue Prince
runners.spotlight: Dropped down the weights last summer and he has rediscovered his spark this time around, following third-placed efforts here and at Goodwood with a comfortable Salisbury success (all over 7f); probably stays this far but he has yet to be placed in his six 1m starts so it will be breaking new ground for him to win today.
runners.comment: Arrives in top form; 1m probably fine but he hasn't shone in previous attempts at the trip

runners.name: Spangled Mac
runners.spotlight: Three good runs over the winter (two of them from five runs in Bahrain) but he has struggled in two 7f runs back in Britain since April; handicapper slow to relent and this step up to 1m isn't sure to spark a revival.
runners.comment: Struggled in two runs back in Britain; tackles 1m for the first time on his 46th start

runners.name: Kitaro Kich
runners.spotlight: Ran well over C&D last October and he was well backed when winning over 7f back here five days later; below his best at Chelmsford one day later; history doesn't suggest coming back from a break is the time to catch him.
runners.comment: Two good course runs (1m/7f) last October; 259-day absence the negative
runners.owner: J Machale, S Tucker & S Jewell

runners.name: First Ambition
runners.spotlight: Won his first two starts for Karl Burke last year but he hasn't kicked on from his handicap debut second (1m, good to soft) last September (RPR 95); sold for 22,000gns in April; drops in class but not a solid option.
runners.comment: Progress stalled for Karl Burke; sold 22,000gns since latest; down in class but risky

races.race_name: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div II)

runners.name: Zatsgood
runners.spotlight: Ex-French gelding who made the breakthrough when landing the odds on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) in May; flopped in his follow-up bid at Leicester but that was at 1m2f and the tacky ground may have been an issue; now drops back in trip and it wouldn't be a surprise if this unexposed 4yo resumes his progress.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo who had an excuse latest and could resume his progress back down in trip

runners.name: Renewal
runners.spotlight: Record of 4232 in novice/maiden events and was a good second on her handicap debut at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) in October; looks interesting on that form but she's struggled in both subsequent runs (1m, good/soft) and needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo but she's struggled in last two runs and needs to get back on track

runners.name: Vincent Rocks
runners.spotlight: Won at Nottingham (8.3f, good to soft) and Southwell (1m, AW) in his first two starts, but he's struggled in three of his four handicaps including when last of ten at Chester (10.3f) last month; has looked stretched in both attempts at that trip but he needs a major revival back on AW; first-time visor replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Has struggled in three of his four handicaps and others are more convincing

runners.name: Farasi Lane
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner who scored twice at Wolverhampton (8.6f/7f) in March; hasn't threatened in his last four runs but he was respectable fifth over C&D on Monday; this is a quick turnaround but his last win was off this mark and he could be dangerous if he gets the breaks; cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: On last winning mark and was a fair fifth over C&D on Monday; could be dangerous

runners.name: Notimeforchitchat
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been at Kempton and latest was over C&D last June; now 1lb lower than for that success but he's been up and down since and was quiet on his reappearance here in May; comes with risks attached.
runners.comment: All three wins have been at Kempton but he's not easy to predict; risks attached
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late G Kennington

runners.name: Billy Mill
runners.spotlight: Eight-time AW winner who has finished runner-up over C&D in his last three starts including a close call on his penultimate run; should give it another good shot and he's respected.
runners.comment: Eight-time AW winner who has finished runner-up over C&D in his last three runs; respected

runners.name: The Liffey
runners.spotlight: Dual winner for Aidan O'Brien in his early days; his three wins this year have all been in claimers at Dundalk (6f-1m) but he travelled smoothly before winning with plenty in hand last time; with another new yard now but he remains well treated on old form and is not ruled out back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Won three Dundalk claimers this year; not ruled out back in a handicap for new yard

runners.name: Breakdancer
runners.spotlight: Won AW handicaps at Kempton (7f) and Southwell (1m) last August and he's been in good form in the UAE this winter, including when runner-up at Abu Dhabi (1m, good) on his penultimate run; returns after 111 days off but he's strongly respected back on Polytrack.
runners.comment: Two AW wins in Britain and he was in good form in the UAE this winter; respected on return

runners.name: Atlantis Blue
runners.spotlight: Triple turf winner who is 0-10 on AW but she returned after 202 days off with a respectable fourth over C&D in May; didn't get the best of runs in that race and has possibilities if she can build on that; regular hood is removed.
runners.comment: 0-10 on AW but she reappeared with a fair fourth over C&D in May; could be in the mix

runners.name: Helm Rock
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D in April and he went close on Polytrack and turf in his next two starts; didn't really fire at Newmarket (1m, good) last time but he still looks on a workable mark and has claims if he can get back near best.
runners.comment: Didn't really fire at Newmarket latest but he was knocking on the door before that

races.race_name: Unibet 40,000+ Live Streamed Events Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Master Vintner
runners.spotlight: Won last September on first two starts for yard; disappointing favourite at Newmarket (1m4f, good) on return but second of 13 over C&D (Bulletin fourth) five weeks ago was his best form yet; up 3lb but this race looks less competitive.
runners.comment: Second of 13 over C&D (Bulletin fourth) five weeks ago was his best form; up 3lb
runners.owner: R A Pegum And Paul Anthony Henry

runners.name: Bulletin
runners.spotlight: Extraordinarily consistent sort who is yet to finish out of the frame in 16 starts; Master Vintner and two others proved too strong for him late on over C&D two starts back but that rival is 3lb worse off at today's weights, so they are very closely matched.
runners.comment: Yet to finish out of the frame; very closely matched with Master Vintner judged on C&D run

runners.name: Yaa Min
runners.spotlight: Two wins from six starts since joining Hayley Burton, both over 1m4f on Lingfield Polytrack; dictated the pace on second occasion and he's not been in quite such good form on either start since, albeit with 2m looking a stretch on latest; probably on the premises.
runners.comment: Two 1m4f wins on Lingfield Polytrack; 2m a stretch latest start; probably on the premises

runners.name: Steel Tiger
runners.spotlight: Well treated judged on peak form (AW in spring 2025) but it's a serious concern that his latest start (five weeks ago; made most) so seriously damaged his record of going well when back from a break; that was the first time he's been tongue tied (as he is today) and also his first race at Kempton.
runners.comment: Well treated judged on peak form (AW in spring 2025) but his latest start (C&D) was poor

runners.name: Tripoli Flyer
runners.spotlight: Winner of a bumper and three novice hurdle races, most recently a Grade 2 here in February 2025; chasing didn't work out but he mostly ran with credit in defeat back over hurdles and three Flat races have been thrown in (1m3f here latest) in which he's shown some ability; it's not clear what his ideal Flat trip is but he's now qualified for a Flat handicap mark and it's a highly attractive one compared to his hurdles figure.
runners.comment: He now has a Flat handicap mark and it's highly attractive compared to his hurdles figure

runners.name: Thinthread
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful dual winner (about 1m1f) in France for Andre Fabre in 2025, latterly on Polytrack; sold for 40,000gns in October and gelded; not seen since and needs to hit the ground running on debut for new yard.
runners.comment: Two wins (about 1m1f) in France for Andre Fabre in 2025, latter on Polytrack; off 268 days

runners.name: Max Mayhem
runners.spotlight: Dual Kempton winner in 2023 and he's very well handicapped judged on form as recent as one year ago; Bahrain last winter did not go well for new connections, however, and although there was a whiff of his old form when seventh of 13 over C&D on his British return in May, he tailed off at Epsom ten days later; soft ground might excuse that but there are questions to answer.
runners.comment: A whiff of his old form over C&D on his British return in May, but questions to answer

races.race_name: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Concert
runners.spotlight: Won a Yarmouth maiden (6f, good to firm) for William Haggas last August; has mixed record since but she got back near her best when third at Newcastle (6f, AW) in May; 1lb lower after a short break and she's a player if she can back that up.
runners.comment: Has patchy profile but was placed at Newcastle latest; dangerous if she can back that up

runners.name: Echo Of Faith
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner (7.5f, good) for Jessica Harrington last August but he's finished down the field in three subsequent runs including on handicap/stable debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) two weeks ago; still lightly raced but he needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Hasn't gone on from his maiden win in Ireland and others are more convincing

runners.name: The Lost Sock
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f maiden at Catterick last summer but he's been expensive to follow in handicaps this year; now 9lb lower than for his opening mark but he's been well held in last three runs and needs blinkers to make a difference.
runners.comment: Won 5f maiden last summer but he's been expensive to follow in handicaps; blinkers added

runners.name: Unionville
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding; dead-heated in a Chelmsford maiden (6f, AW) on second run for current yard but he's been well held in two handicaps since; has bit to prove again and overall record record is now 1-7.
runners.comment: Dead-heated at Chelmsford in February but well held back in handicaps since; down the list

runners.name: Arctic Wind
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden for two different yards but he ran into some trouble when a fair third in Ffos Las handicap (5f, good to soft) last month; dropped another 2lb and he could be dangerous on this step back up in trip.
runners.comment: 0-6 but he's on a reduced mark and could be dangerous back up in trip

runners.name: Our Guy
runners.spotlight: Suited by the drop to 6f when scoring on handicap debut at Windsor (good to firm) in May; only 2lb higher here but he lost his action and was pulled up at Windsor two weeks ago; needs to bounce back after that and he still has a bit to prove on AW.
runners.comment: Won at Windsor in May but he lost his action and was pulled up there last time

runners.name: Trinculo
runners.spotlight: Encouraging return when third of ten in a C&D maiden in May and although only third of four at Windsor (6f, good to firm) three weeks later, the winner and fourth have won since; opening mark is no gift but it's still early days and he should have more to offer.
runners.comment: Opening mark is no gift but it's still early days and he should have more to offer

runners.name: Shahik
runners.spotlight: Won a Newcastle maiden (6f, Tapeta) for George Boughey last October, but he's struggled in four runs for new yard this year; continues to drop down the weights but he needs a major revival back on AW; blinkers removed.
runners.comment: Has struggled for new yard this year and he needs a major revival back on AW; headgear off

runners.name: Aigeas
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut in a newcomers' race at Deauville (6.5f, AW) last October, but well held in three starts on turf since; still unexposed but he was withdrawn after getting upset in the stalls at Newbury last week and others are preferred; equipment removed.
runners.comment: Disappointing since debut win at Deauville (6.5f, AW) and others are preferred

runners.name: Perfect Location
runners.spotlight: Has a patchy profile but she improved her record to 2-9 when scoring with cheekpieces added at Goodwood (6f, soft) last month; 5lb higher from wide draw here, but that was a personal best last time and her last visit to Kempton resulted in a good second over C&D; respected.
runners.comment: Has form over C&D and she won in new headgear at Goodwood latest; respected
runners.owner: Mildmay Racing Pl & Only Fillies Rc

runners.name: Night Shining
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise in her qualifying runs including when fourth of 11 at Chester (7f, good) in May; has shaped as though this drop back in trip could suit and she needs watching in market on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Showed promise in her qualifying runs and she needs watching in market on handicap debut

runners.name: Evenepoel
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth on Lingfield debut in December before a last-gasp win over 6f at Chelmsford (7-2) the following month; well held in final run during the winter but he raced wide in that 7f novice; returns after 162 days off but he's still unexposed and needs a close back in trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Chelmsford maiden winner and he needs a close look back at this trip on handicap debut

races.race_name: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Initial Blue
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner (made all both times), including when beating Travel Agent in April; below his best on turf next time but he's been placed on the AW in both starts since; contender.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who has been placed on the AW on his last two outings; contender

runners.name: Travel Agent
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner, most recently at Lingfield (6f) in April off 2lb lower; well held at Yarmouth on his last two starts but has an excuse three weeks ago (reared leaving the stalls); returning to the AW could suit.
runners.comment: Three-time AW winner; had an excuse last time having reared leaving the stalls

runners.name: Diamond Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Five-time AW winner who went close at Lingfield (5f) in March and ran well there over 6f in April, finishing third of five (beaten a length); never got involved there 28 days ago but he could go well returned to 6f.
runners.comment: Five-time AW winner; never got involved at Lingfield (5f) last time but could go well

runners.name: Brazen Idol
runners.spotlight: Missed 2025 but ran well when second of seven over this C&D in March on his first start for 702 days; struggled on his next two starts but returned to form when going close at Ffos Las (5f) nine days ago; this dual C&D will be suited by the return to this trip and has strong claims.
runners.comment: Went close at Ffos Las (5f) nine days ago and holds strong claims returned to this trip

runners.name: Invincible Speed
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner, most recently at Lingfield (6f) in January off the same mark as today's; left the impression he's still in form on soft ground at Salisbury 23 days ago and has place claims back on the AW.
runners.comment: Four-time AW winner who left the impression he's still in form last time; place claims

runners.name: Massimo Blue
runners.spotlight: C&D winner early last year when trained by Phil McEntee and his other success came on the AW at Southwell in October; reported to have lost both shoes when fifth at Newbury (6f, good to firm) nine days ago and may come on for that first start since January; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: C&D winner last year; reported to have lost both shoes last time; cheekpieces now applied

runners.name: Balon d'Or
runners.spotlight: All three wins came on turf when trained by Hugo Palmer, the most recent at Chester in September; 0-12 on the AW but he did post a solid effort when a 1l fourth of seven at Lingfield (5f, AW) 28 days ago; steps back up in trip.
runners.comment: Three-time winner for Hugo Palmer; 0-12 on the AW but posted a solid effort last time

runners.name: Serenity Dream
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in January off a 1lb higher mark than today's and he's posted some good efforts since, including when fifth of 18 in a 0-80 at Ascot in May; beaten about 3l when sixth at Goodwood (7f, soft) 25 days ago and now drops in grade.
runners.comment: C&D winner in January off 1lb higher; beaten 3l at Goodwood latest and now drops in grade

runners.name: Newsreader
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner over 7f for his previous stable but was third over this trip at Windsor in September; well held in both starts since returning from a break and first-time blinkers now replace cheekpieces; dropped another 3lb.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner over 7f; well held in both starts this year; new headgear tried

runners.name: Dannick
runners.spotlight: 15-race maiden but he did finish runner-up on three occasions last year (twice on the AW); however, he's been down the field in three starts this year and drops back in trip with a bit to prove; first-time visor replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 0-15 but runner-up on three occasions; however, down the field in three starts this year

runners.name: Express Train
runners.spotlight: Won a Leicester novice (6f, good to firm) last July and he was third of nine in a handicap at Newcastle (6f, AW) in January; down the field on his final start for Andrew Balding and now makes his stable debut (bought for 10,000gns) after 153 days off; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Down the field on his final start for Andrew Balding and now makes his stable debut

runners.name: Giorgio M
runners.spotlight: 3-35, with the latest success coming at Lingfield (7f, AW) in December off a mark 8lb higher than today's; never featured over this trip at Wolverhampton 31 days ago and better is needed.
runners.comment: 8lb below his last winning mark but never featured at Wolverhampton last month

races.race_name: Sequoia Hair & Beauty Group Handicap

runners.name: Fanciulla Del West
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly who has improved with each run, last time finishing nicely clear of the rest when beaten a length on handicap debut (1m2f, good; 9-2); raised 3lb but that's not the end of the world.
runners.comment: Well-bred filly who has improved with each run, beaten 1l on handicap debut latest

runners.name: Morcar
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner who is 3lb beneath his last winning mark; however, he hasn't offered all that much in three runs following a break and wind surgery.
runners.comment: He hasn't offered all that much in three runs following a break and wind surgery

runners.name: Ablon
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced filly; runner-up at Kempton (1m) in February before making all in a 7f novice back there two weeks later; bred to stay this longer trip well and her best days could still be ahead of her.
runners.comment: Novice winner in February; bred for this far; best days could still be ahead of her
runners.owner: Mrs C Fruhbeck and Denford Stud

runners.name: Brighlee
runners.spotlight: Second to a subsequent Listed winner in August (1m, good to firm) but disappointed in October after a break and hasn't got going this season, including when hooded for handicap debut at Sandown a month ago; downgraded here but with a bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Interesting on some form but a bit to prove now after a quiet handicap debut

runners.name: Harlington
runners.spotlight: A winner on turf but has a superior strike-rate on the AW including a hat-trick earlier this year; only four ran when third at Yarmouth last time and that was under a 5lb claimer; could be vulnerable.
runners.comment: Only four ran when third at Yarmouth last time and that was under a 5lb claimer

runners.name: Home Hero
runners.spotlight: 0-5; both his handicaps have been at Chester (1m2f; good then good to soft); the third was encouraging and then reportedly unsuited by ground that connections described as tacky; cheekpieces go on this time and hard to discount.
runners.comment: Tacky ground reportedly to blame last time; chance on his previous run

runners.name: Madjid
runners.spotlight: Bang there on his handicap debut at Doncaster in April (1m2f, good to firm) but two lesser runs have followed under similar conditions; capable but couldn't be confident about him.
runners.comment: Bang there on his handicap debut at Doncaster in April but lesser runs have followed
runners.owner: Dr Jamal Ahmadzadeh & Mrs D Swinburn

runners.name: Rossa Raheen
runners.spotlight: Had two races for David Menuisier and three for current trainer; nearly won over C&D in May despite not getting the best of runs and soft ground was reportedly unsuitable next time; should run well.
runners.comment: Soft ground no good last time and had previously gone close over C&D

runners.name: Kimeko Glory
runners.spotlight: Won three times at 1m2f (good/good to firm) last year; 0-6 since moving yards and below her best in last four races, the last two in blinkers that are now removed; on a useful mark but a revival is needed.
runners.comment: 0-6 since moving yards and below her best in last four races; blinkers removed

runners.name: Perfect Scoundrel
runners.spotlight: Hasn't had a lot of racing and probably his best effort yet in handicaps when a 4l third at Goodwood a month ago (1m1f, good to soft); he still doesn't look on that generous a mark.
runners.comment: Bit better when third at Goodwood but he still doesn't look on that generous a mark
runners.owner: Mrs F Cotton, Mr & Mrs P Conway

runners.name: James Choice
runners.spotlight: 65,000gns breeze-up buy who shaped with promise last season in 7f/1m races won by smart horses; absent since October but potentially interesting on this handicap debut now raised in distance; the market could be enlightening.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise last season in 7f/1m races won by smart horses; handicap debut

races.race_name: Gracelands EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Topaz
runners.spotlight: 13-2 at Newmarket (6f, good) in May and came from rear to give the favourite a scare in closing stages; never showed when 20-1 for Group 3 at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm) 13 days ago but the promising debut cannot yet be forgotten.
runners.comment: Never showed when 20-1 for Group 3 at Royal Ascot but her debut had been promising

runners.name: Avalon Queen
runners.spotlight: By Masar out of a French 1m2f-1m4f winner (including Group 2; RPR 111); that strongly suggests much longer trips than this but she made a bright debut, briefly threatening 1f out, when fourth of 12 in maiden here (6.5f, good to firm; 22-1) one month ago, so has to be considered.
runners.comment: Pedigree suggests much further but she made a bright debut here (6.5f, good to firm)

runners.name: Parvenue Star
runners.spotlight: 12,000gns foal, 62,000gns yearling; sixth foal; Starman half-sister to 1m-1m2f Flat (including 2yo; RPR 88) and 2m hurdle winner Into Battle; dam unraced half-sister to winners Johann Zoffany (Australian 1m6f Group 2) and Crown Choice (useful 6f/7f); powerful yard also runs Gravastar.
runners.comment: 62,000gns yearling; Starman filly from powerful yard which also runs Gravastar

runners.name: Bymiddaytomorrow
runners.spotlight: Has run in two 6f novice events at Windsor, showing some improvement when rallying for fifth of ten on good to soft 24 days ago but that suggests she needs markedly better still; jockey bookings suggest that Royal Message is the stable first string.
runners.comment: Rallying fifth of ten at at Windsor (6f, good to soft) but needs markedly better still

runners.name: Fillipas
runners.spotlight: 17-2, no match for a red-hot favourite (subsequent Listed winner) in five-runner novice at Bath (5f, good) 19 days ago but drew 5l clear of the rest, showing significant ability and promise.
runners.comment: 17-2, no match for a good winner at Bath (5f, good; five ran) but 5l clear of the rest

runners.name: Gravastar
runners.spotlight: By a 6f/7f performer but there's stacks of stamina on the dam's side; 16-1, never dangerous from out the back when 8l fourth of eight behind an odds-on stablemate in maiden at Goodwood (6f, soft) three weeks ago, hampered at the start; probably capable of a lot better but whether she's sharp enough to eclipse all these rivals over 6f on good to firm is another question.
runners.comment: Probably capable of a lot better than on debut but stiff task to be sharp enough for this

runners.name: Nuit d'Eclair
runners.spotlight: 280,000gns yearling; seventh foal; Night Of Thunder half-sister to winners Attagirl (5f/6f 2yo including Listed; RPR 98), Skiathos (7f 2yo; 78) and Symbol Of Hope (5f/5.7f; 75); dam Italian 5f-6f 2yo winner (including Listed; 99), half-sister to Group-placed 5f 2yo winner; late foal but needs a close look.
runners.comment: 280,000gns yearling by Night Of Thunder; late foal but needs a close look

runners.name: Art Of Life
runners.spotlight: 70,000gns yearling by Palace Pier; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Genealogy (1m 2yo; RPR 93), Pianoforte (5f; 86), Shahik (6f AW 2yo; 78) and Artifact (US 1m/8.5f); dam Group-placed 6f 2yo winner (95) out of half-sister to 1,000 Guineas winner Finsceal Beo; same connections had a second-time-out 2yo winner on Monday.
runners.comment: 70,000gns yearling by Palace Pier; connections had a second-time-out 2yo winner on Monday

runners.name: Kiah
runners.spotlight: 38,000gns yearling by Australia; ninth foal; sister to 1m2f 2yo winner Anzac Day (RPR 99), half-sister to four winners including Merveillo (10.5f-1m4f including Listed; 99) and Ouezy (10.7f; 98); dam 1m3f winner, closely related to 1m 2yo Group 1/1m4f Grade 1 winner Ectot; pedigree suggests strongly that she is one for much further at a later stage.
runners.comment: Useful pedigree suggests strongly that she is one for much further at a later stage

runners.name: Lola De Valence
runners.spotlight: Third foal; Havana Grey half-sister to 7f winner Back In Black (including 2yo; RPR 101); dam 7f 2yo winner (78), half-sister to 1m Listed winner Awesometank, out of 6f 2yo winner; 12-1 but upset in stalls when withdrawn from 6f Windsor novice (Bymiddaytomorrow 5-1) just over three weeks ago.
runners.comment: 12-1 but upset in stalls when withdrawn from a 6f Windsor novice (Bymiddaytomorrow 5-1)

runners.name: Marsala
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; Mehmas half-sister to 5f-7f winner Palmar Bay (including 2yo; RPR 100); dam 6f/7f winner (including 2yo/Listed; 106), sister to 7f 2yo and 1m3f Listed winner Dibayani; one to note from a leading yard.
runners.comment: Mehmas half-sister to 5f-7f winner Palmar Bay (including 2yo; RPR 100); from a strong yard

runners.name: Spirit Of Progress
runners.spotlight: 5,000gns foal; by Showcasing; third foal; dam 1m-1m4f winner (including AW Listed; RPR 107), half-sister to winners War Diary (9.7f/1m4f) and St Saviour (1m2f Flat/useful 2m hurdle), out of 1m-winning half-sister to five-time 1m Group 1 winner Attraction; trainer not known for 2yos.
runners.comment: 5,000gns foal; by Showcasing out of 1m-1m4f winner (RPR 107); trainer not known for 2yos

runners.name: Royal Message
