runners.spotlight: No Nay Never half-sister to 7f winner Champion Again Ole (RPR 86); dam 6f winner (including 2yo/Group 2), from talented family; 16-1 when just over 7l fifth of eight in maiden at Leicester (6f, good to firm) five weeks ago, outpaced at halfway, but that was a hot race and she should improve; yard runs two and jockey bookings suggest that she is the stable first string.
runners.comment: 16-1 fifth of eight at Leicester (6f, good to firm) but in hot race and she should improve
runners.owner: Lord Lloyd Webber & W S Farish

runners.name: Perfect Hope
runners.spotlight: 25-1 and backward when 11l sixth of ten at Windsor (6f, good to soft) just over three weeks ago, struggling to stay in touch from halfway and Bymiddaytomorrow 8l ahead; should improve but can't be fancied just yet.
runners.comment: Bymiddaytomorrow was 8l ahead at Windsor; should improve but can't be fancied just yet

races.race_name: Fidelity Energy Green Future Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings) (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Alaskan Bear
runners.spotlight: Both runs over 6f on good ground in races won by Ballydoyle hotpots; second to the smart Confucius on debut and then fourth in the Group 3 Marble Hill won by subsequent Coventry winner Great Barrier Reef; the third has since gone close in another Group 3; faces promising opposition here but he sets the bar quite high.
runners.comment: Has experience and sets the bar high on the strength of his fourth in a Group 3

runners.name: Magi Melchior
runners.spotlight: £45,000 yearling; second foal out of an unraced half-sister to useful winners New Kingdom (7f/1m) and Cruyff Turn (7f/1m); interesting newcomer from a yard that's won five of the last ten runnings of this race.
runners.comment: £45,000 yearling; interesting newcomer from a yard that has a fine record in this race

runners.name: Noahs Gold
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to 7.3f 2yo winner Torbay (RPR 54); dam 6f 2yo/7f AW winner (95), half-sister to 8.6f winner Dwight D; jumps-orientated yard is 1-22 with 2yos the last five seasons.
runners.comment: Newcomer; jumps-orientated yard is 1-22 with 2yos the last five seasons

runners.name: Tumishi
runners.spotlight: 180,000gns breeze-up 2yo; 11-1 when beaten a shade over 3l at Nottingham (6f, good); formlines through the first two give him something to find with Alaskan Bear but inmates from this yard usually improve with racing.
runners.comment: 180,000gns breeze-up 2yo; 11-1 when beaten a shade over 3l at Nottingham (6f, good)

runners.name: Pride Of Toledo
runners.spotlight: £100,000 yearling; seventh foal; half-brother to winners Aloha Star (5f/6f, including Group 2; RPR 103), Eye Smiling (6f; 73) and Zain Master (Italian 1m3f/12.5f Flat and 2m/2m1f hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Battalion; newcomers from this yard are always of interest, especially here.
runners.comment: £100,000 yearling; newcomers from this yard are always of interest, especially here

runners.name: Off The Peg
runners.spotlight: 60,000gns foal, 150,000gns yearling; brother to 5f 2yo Listed winner His Majesty (RPR 100) and half-brother to winners Coill Avon (6f; 93) and Spanish (1m; 90); dam a very smart 6f-1m winner; could be interesting.
runners.comment: 60,000gns foal, 150,000gns yearling; brother to 5f 2yo Listed winner His Majesty (RPR 100)
runners.owner: B Dickson, P Cook & R Rexton

runners.name: Coatimundi
runners.spotlight: 68,000euros yearling; second foal; half-brother to 6f-1m winner Fred On Fire (RPR 82); dam 1m4f winner (85); best to let the betting guide.
runners.comment: 68,000euros yearling; second foal; half-brother to 6f-1m winner Fred On Fire (RPR 82)

runners.name: Hackpen Hill
runners.spotlight: 75,000gns yearling; first foal; dam placed 1m3f/1m4f AW (RPR 83), half-sister to useful 1m2f 2yo/1m4f winner Galactic Jack; one of two newcomers for the yard and Shakwaa appeals more.
runners.comment: 75,000gns yearling; one of two newcomers for the yard and Shakwaa appeals more

runners.name: Jimtrott
runners.spotlight: 110,000gns yearling; only 9-2 for his debut here over slightly further and went down narrowly having had every chance (on good to soft); presumably well thought of and expected to take a step forward second time out.
runners.comment: Only 9-2 for his debut here over slightly further and went down narrowly

runners.name: Secano
runners.spotlight: 36,000gns foal, 80,000gns yearling; tenth foal; brother to winners Brayden Star (7.5f-1m4f, including Australian Group 2; RPR 109) and Prince Ali (7f-1m4f; 71), half-brother to four winners including Wentwood (1m-1m4f including Australian Group 3; 102); may need further and likely best watched unless market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: May need further and likely best watched unless market suggests otherwise
runners.owner: Oti Racing P Inglett S De Zoete &partner

runners.name: Shakwaa
runners.spotlight: 90,000gns foal, 210,000gns yearling; first foal of a US 1m/1m1f turf winner (RPR 100) and half-sister to Listed-placed 5f/5.5f winner Air Force Jet; looks a likely type on paper.
runners.comment: 90,000gns foal, 210,000gns yearling; first foal of a US 1m/1m1f turf winner (RPR 100)

races.race_name: World Cup Super Boosts At BetVictor Handicap

runners.name: Mercury Day
runners.spotlight: Won twice at around 1m two years ago; close third over 6f at Newmarket on final run of last season and signs of edging back into form when beaten 4l in a Class 2 at York 20 days ago (6f); returning to further off a handy mark, she's one to consider.
runners.comment: Signs of edging back into form when beaten 4l in a Class 2 at York 20 days ago

runners.name: Best Rate
runners.spotlight: 7f AW novice winner at two; 0-16 in handicaps but yet another sound effort in defeat last time at Newmarket (7f, good); handicapped about right but one of these can come his way.
runners.comment: Only 1-19 overall; handicapped about right but one of these can come his way

runners.name: Gallant
runners.spotlight: Won two AW races for Andrew Balding whom he left for 60,000gns; he's not always looked entirely straightforward but he was less than 2l away last time at Newmarket (7f, good) after being in rear at one point; Tom Marquand takes over now; respected.
runners.comment: Came from behind to be less than 2l off the winner last time at Newmarket (7f)

runners.name: Commander Of Life
runners.spotlight: Exposed four-time winner; has run a number of good races this season and Doncaster last time was one of those, as he did well to finish so close after missing the break; not without hope.
runners.comment: A number of good races this season and Doncaster last time was one of those

runners.name: Herculeus
runners.spotlight: Last year's wins were at about 1m and hasn't raced over 7f since his debut; quiet this season after a gelding operation and others appeal more; cheekpieces, in which he has form figures of 100, now return.
runners.comment: Last year's wins were at about 1m and hasn't raced over 7f since his debut

runners.name: Slipper Time
runners.spotlight: Novice winner who was close up in a C&D handicap last August off 1lb higher; major claims if able to repeat that form, which hasn't been the case in three subsequent races.
runners.comment: Novice winner who was close up in a C&D handicap last August off 1lb higher; quiet since

runners.name: Manly Fireball
runners.spotlight: Hasn't raced on turf since his debut here but now contests only his second handicap after winning a 6f novice from the front at Wolverhampton a month ago; the odds-on runner-up has since been placed on his handicap debut at York; possibilities over a trip he stays.
runners.comment: Comfortably off the mark in a 6f AW novice last time; this is only his second handicap

runners.name: Kennington
runners.spotlight: It wasn't a strong 7f novice that he won on good ground at Lingfield but he came clear at the finish and brings potential into handicaps; one to be interested in.
runners.comment: Clearcut winner of a 7f novice at Lingfield and brings potential into handicaps
runners.owner: Mrs M T Bevan & A Bevan

runners.name: Annastarzy
runners.spotlight: On a hat-trick after improved efforts to win over 1m on good ground at Thirsk and Goodwood (both under Joe Leavy); she was all out last time in a race that panned out favourably but a 4lb rise was fair and this is only her eighth race, albeit her first not over a mile.
runners.comment: Only had seven races and won her last two, both at 1m; all out last time and up 4lb

runners.name: Melvin Udall
runners.spotlight: 7f novice winner for Ed Walker; made a successful stable debut off 4lb lower in March (7f, good to soft) but hasn't kicked on from there.
runners.comment: Successful stable debut in March but hasn't kicked on from there
runners.owner: K Mills, J Brown, S Barley

runners.name: Euphonia
runners.spotlight: Her two 7f wins (good/soft) came at a cost mark-wise and she could finish only sixth at Sandown; this is her first time at this loftier level and she needs a career best by some margin.
runners.comment: This is her first time at this loftier level and she needs a career best by some margin

runners.name: Stratocracy
runners.spotlight: Four wins divided between turf and the AW; 7lb above his highest winning mark and appeared to have no excuses when fifth of 14 at Wetherby four weeks ago (7f, good to firm).
runners.comment: Appeared to have no excuses when fifth of 14 at Wetherby four weeks ago
runners.owner: Total Asbestos Solutions Ltd & A Frost

runners.name: Huscal
runners.spotlight: He's gone 0-12 in handicaps but is edging down the weights and ran well to finish fourth of ten at Windsor (1m, good to firm) last time (form has been franked); this season's runs have been in Class 3s so this is a drop in grade.
runners.comment: He's gone 0-12 in handicaps but ran well in the latest and he's eased in grade

runners.name: Splash
runners.spotlight: Her second in a 7f novice at Newmarket last October is very much a standout; 11-1 and beat only two home in a 7f handicap there in May.
runners.comment: Her second in a 7f novice at Newmarket last October is very much a standout

runners.name: Mr Ubiquitous
runners.spotlight: Back from a break to claim career win number six on the Kempton AW (7f) in March but then disappointed at Yarmouth (off this 3lb higher mark); consistent in the main since sent into handicaps and breaks tend to do him good.
runners.comment: Below par last time but he's 6-17 overall and breaks tend to do him good

races.race_name: Local IQ Handicap

runners.name: Thanos
runners.spotlight: Progressive; 2-2 since fitted with cheekpieces, supplementing a convincing 7f Lingfield win with another over 1m at Chepstow (good to firm, 4-6 favourite); this is a sterner test from 10lb higher than last time but, with fast ground to suit, it'll be no surprise if he's up to the challenge.
runners.comment: Progressive; 2-2 since fitted with cheekpieces; raised 10lb since Chepstow; major player.

runners.name: Lucky Luna
runners.spotlight: Made all at Salisbury (7f novice, soft; 11-1) three weeks ago; it isn't easy to know exactly what to make of that - runner-up was a 400-1 shot - but, closely related to the 1m2f winner Capla Lazarus, she's open to improvement on handicap debut over 1m.
runners.comment: Made all at Salisbury (7f novice) three weeks ago; tackles a new trip on handicap debut.

runners.name: Del Corso
runners.spotlight: Still seeking breakthrough (0-6), going closest when third at Nottingham (8.5f, good; 4-1 favourite) in April; soft ground given as the reason for a less-convincing effort at Lingfield (1m1f; fourth of seven, 5-2 favourite) latest.
runners.comment: 0-6; third at Nottingham (8.5f; 4-1 fav) in April; soft ground excuse latest.

runners.name: Drymee
runners.spotlight: With Joseph Parr when an eyecatching fourth at Leicester (7f; 150-1) last September; produced only a fair opening effort for this trainer, but stepped up to score at Lingfield (7.5f, soft; 2-1 favourite) a fortnight later; interesting to see what he makes of handicapping now with Tom Marquand booked.
runners.comment: Lingfield winner (7.5f, soft; 2-1 fav); interesting to see what he makes of handicapping.

runners.name: Venturing
runners.spotlight: Didn't offer much on his first two starts for Donnacha O'Brien, and only marginally better when a staying-on fourth at Dundalk (1m maiden; 6-1, beaten over 12l) in February; best watched on first start for Tony Carroll; handicap debut.
runners.comment: Didn't offer much in three for D. O' Brien; best watched on first start for T. Carroll.

races.race_name: Pump Technology Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Over Spiced
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; off the mark for the year with a narrow win at Bath (5f, good) in May; held her form since and her latest Sandown third came in a higher grade; Ashley Lewis knows her well and she's a leading contender.
runners.comment: Bath win in May and held her form since; latest Sandown third came in a 0-80; solid claims

runners.name: Truly Glamorous
runners.spotlight: On the up for new stable this year, fourth at Windsor on his return in May before getting off the mark in a Class 6 at Windsor (5f, good to firm) 17 days ago; still looked to be learning his job on that occasion and a 5lb rise is unlikely to prove beyond him; apprentice rider Harry Vigors making a big name for himself; lots to like.
runners.comment: Unexposed and improving 3yo; 5lb rise for his Windsor win is unlikely to prove beyond him

runners.name: Life After Love
runners.spotlight: On the up on AW in the spring, winning three 5f handicaps at Southwell; signs that the handicapper is catching up more recently and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Three 5f wins at Southwell this year; handicapper may have caught up now

runners.name: Faustus
runners.spotlight: Twice a winner over C&D, including the corresponding event 12 months ago when rated 11lb higher; back to form when second at Windsor ten days ago, failing to hold the late thrust of Just Jump; his supporters should get a run for their money but he could just prove vulnerable late on.
runners.comment: Won this race last year off 11lb higher; picked off late by Just Jump at Windsor latest

runners.name: Merrimack
runners.spotlight: Three good turf runs to his name this summer, all for today's apprentice Chloe Lyons and including C&D; no obvious reason why he wouldn't go well once again.
runners.comment: Three solid turf runs for this rider this summer, including C&D; should be in the mix

runners.name: Just Jump
runners.spotlight: Shaped well on his stable debut at Lingfield (6f, AW) in February but hamstrung by slow starts on his next three starts; further blotted his copybook when refusing to race at Windsor in May; well backed back at Windsor (5f, good to firm) ten days ago and again not foot perfect leaving the stalls but he overcame some adversity (stumbled early and had to be switched in the straight) to win going away; little doubt he has the ability to defy his penalty but there are risks attached.
runners.comment: Better than this mark but the stalls are an issue; could follow up Windsor win but risky

runners.name: Havana Jag
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in nine runs for Dylan Cunha; may come on for her recent stable debut (after 102 days off) but she needs to.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden; needs to leave recent stable debut behind her

runners.name: Artista
runners.spotlight: Won twice as a 2yo; she has found the going tougher this time around but the handicapper is relenting and this should be more suitable; others still look more appealing; stable runs two.
runners.comment: Two wins as a 2yo but she has found it tougher going this year; dropping down the weights

runners.name: Nifty
runners.spotlight: She made a bright start to her career last summer, winning a Chelmsford maiden and a Salisbury novice (both 6f); struggling in 2026 though and opposable despite her reduced mark.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins as a 2yo; struggling this year and has to prove she has trained on
